lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Not good at all my friend. It's Elena, she's been taken...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Taken? By who?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: The Knights of Ardougne. They claimed she was inciting rebellion against the king. Apparently she's been going around telling people that the plague isn't real...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear... This is a problem.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Is there anything you can do to help us? It feels wrong to ask given what you've done for us before, but we're desperate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure this is all just a misunderstanding. Let me try talking to the king.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Thank you friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't help I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Edmond tells me you're helping Elena again. Thank you so much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day my lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Hello [player name]. What can I do for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Elena...

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: We've been through this already. I will not have the girl freed. She is a threat to Ardougne and will remain imprisoned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I guess I should let her father know.

sections.title: Talking to the Knight of Ardougne
Climbing down the prison stairs without knight armour

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Sorry citizen. Only Knights of Ardougne are allowed in the prison.

sections.title: Talking to Knight without knight armour

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Hello there citizen. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's down these stairs?

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: These stairs lead to the Ardougne Prison. It's home to thieves, murderers and everything in between.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds lovely. Can I go in?

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: I'm afraid not citizen. Only the Knights of Ardougne are allowed into the prison and I can see that you are no knight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day my lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Hello [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to talk about Elena, apparently she's been imprisoned.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: That she has. She was making some heinous accusations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That the plague is fake?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... But you and I both know that she's right, the plague is fake.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: That's not the point. As I've explained to you before, the plague is a necessary evil. We can't have her exposing the truth and risking everything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But King Tyras is dead, what exactly are we risking?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: My brother may be dead but the Dark Lord is still a threat. Until that threat is gone, the plague must remain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you won't free Elena?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: No. I can't take any risks. Now I'm afraid I have business to attend to. You should be on your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I guess I should let her father know.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after completing the disguise

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: You need to disguise yourself as a Knight of Ardougne. Get yourself a steel platebody, some steel platelegs and a steel full helm. The plume on the helm will need dying red and you'll need a tabard for the platebody.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: My wife should be able to help you with the tabard. You'll have to check with her to see what she needs for it. Come back to me once you have everything and we'll discuss what to do next.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Hello there. Have you spoken to the king yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He won't be freeing her I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: But why? I know all this plague business has got her a bit emotional, but can't he see that she's just upset?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said she was a threat to Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: A threat? But she's no danger to anyone. Why does he think that way?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the thing is...

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... She's right. The plague is a lie. That's why the king had her imprisoned. He was afraid she'd expose the truth.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: The plague is a lie? What do you mean the plague is a lie?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I have some explaining to do.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: You most certainly do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it all started after I freed Elena from West Ardougne. As you can imagine, she was still keen on helping the plague victims and asked me to help her find a cure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We sent some samples to her old teacher, Guidor in Varrock. He ran some tests and found out that the plague isn't actually real. I confronted the king on this and he admitted it.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: But why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king told me that his brother, King Tyras, was corrupted by the Dark Lord while exploring the lands to the west.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As Tyras was the king of West Ardougne, King Lathas decided to cordon the city off to keep the rest of the world safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The plague was the excuse he gave for the quarantine.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: But that doesn't make any sense. Why not just tell the truth? Why lie to everyone and make up a plague? Something doesn't seem right about it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you're right about that. Turns out King Lathas was lying about that as well. But I'll get to that in a minute.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: After confronting the king, he recruited me and tasked me with helping him stop Tyras. He had me travel through the Underground Pass into the elven forests of Tirannwn.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Elves? I wasn't even sure they existed anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well Tirannwn is full of them. In fact, they're currently in the middle of a civil war.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: An elven civil war? I'm going to need a drink after all of this. So what happened next?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I met with an ally of King Lathas, an elf called Lord Iorwerth. He's the leader of one of the elven factions. With his help, I was successful in assassinating King Tyras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With the job done, I returned to Ardougne. However, on my way back, I was intercepted by an elf named Arianwyn, the leader of the rebel elves opposing Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He revealed the truth to me, that King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth are in fact the evil ones. Together they intend to summon this Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Just when I thought this story couldn't get any worse. The king is evil? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: From what I can tell, King Lathas hated how his father split the kingdom, giving half to him and half to his brother. He wanted his brother dead from the start.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not only that, he also wants to reclaim Camelot from King Arthur. I think he's hoping that this Dark Lord will help him.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: All of this, just because Lathas wants more land? What about this Lord Iorwerth, what's in it for him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think he wants to use the Dark Lord to defeat the rebel elves and end the civil war. Apparently, it's been going on for hundreds of years.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I see, this still doesn't explain why the plague was made up though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well since I learnt the truth, I've been helping the rebel elves fight back against King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I recently discovered that Lord Iorwerth believes the secrets to summoning the Dark Lord lie within the Temple of Light beneath Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The plague was made up to hide their mining operation beneath the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All the mourners are actually Iorwerth elves in disguise and the people who they claim have died of the plague are actually getting sent into the mines.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Goodness me Elena, what have you gotten yourself into?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Thank you for telling me the truth [player name]. I just wish you had told me sooner.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: If even half of what you say about King Lathas is true, then my daughter is in great danger. We need to help her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have something in mind?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I do. They're holding Elena in the city prison beneath the castle. You'll need to disguise yourself as a Knight of Ardougne, infiltrate the prison and find a way to free her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why me?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: As much as I hate to admit it, I'm far too old to pass as a knight. It will have to be you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get this disguise?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: You'll need to get a steel platebody, some steel platelegs and a steel full helm. The plume on the helm will need dying red and you'll need a tabard for the platebody.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: My wife should be able to help you with the tabard. You'll have to check with her to see what she needs for it. Come back to me once you have everything and we'll discuss what to do next.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: You'll need to get a steel platebody, some steel platelegs and a steel full helm. The plume on the helm will need dying red and you'll need a tabard for the platebody.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: My wife should be able to help you with the tabard. You'll have to check with her to see what she needs for it. Come back to me once you have everything and we'll discuss what to do next.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena again without the required items

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hello there. Do you have the silk and purple dye yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll go and get them.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after getting the required items

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hello there. Do you have the silk and purple dye yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Here you go, one tabard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't have them.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Well I can't make your tabard then.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after getting the tabard

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Thank you so much for helping Elena. It means so much to Edmond and I.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Edmond tells me you're helping Elena again. Thank you so much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem. Do you think you could give me a hand with something?

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to make a Knight of Ardougne tabard.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hmmm, well that shouldn't be too hard for me to do. Could you bring me some silk and some purple dye please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Luckily for us, I have them both right here.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hmm, that is lucky.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Here you go, one tabard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't have them.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Well I can't make your tabard then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after completing the disguise

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Take your disguise and head down to the prison to see what you can find out. You'll find it beneath the castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished the disguise.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Well you definitely look the part. Those guards will be totally fooled. Now get yourself down to the prison and see if you can find a way to free Elena. You'll find it beneath the castle.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Excellent, let's have a look at it.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Yes, this should be perfect. Those guards will be totally fooled. Now get yourself down to the prison and see if you can find a way to free Elena. You'll find it beneath the castle.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Yes, this should be perfect. Those platelegs are a bit dirty though, let me give them a polish for you.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: There you go. Now get yourself down to the prison and see if you can find a way to free Elena. You'll find it beneath the castle.

sections.title: Talking to the Knight of Ardougne

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Hello there fellow knight. Isn't today a wonderful day to be a Knight of Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean it's okay I suppose. The constant fear of some random adventurer murdering you and the fact that people are constantly picking your pockets isn't great. But hey ho, at least the benefits are good.

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: That they are fellow knight, that they are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, can I go down these stairs?

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Of course you can! You're a Knight of Ardougne! There is no place where you can't go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the Knight of Ardougne again

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Good to see you [Tim/Sue/Sam].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's [Tim/Sue/Sam]?

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: You are?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I am [Tim/Sue/Sam].

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Hmm... Is your head working okay today [Tim/Sue/Sam]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, my head has never been better.

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: If you say so...

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Hey you!

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... Err... [Tim/Sue/Sam]?

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: [Tim/Sue/Sam]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... [Tim/Sue/Sam]...

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Huh...

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: You don't look much like a [Tim/Sue/Sam].

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: What are you doing here... [Tim/Sue/Sam]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just here on guard duty.

lines.speaker: Knight of Ardougne
lines.text: Hmm... Very well. As you were... [Tim/Sue/Sam]...

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena it's me, [player name].

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name]? What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rescuing you of course. Your father told me you were here.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Oh thank goodness. It's awful in here. The smell from that mugger...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened Elena? How did you end up here?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Oh it's quite the tale, but let's save it for until I'm out of here. What's your plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I err... I don't really have one.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You don't have a plan? But aren't you meant to be an adventurer? How do you normally do this sort of thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, I usually just make it up as I go along.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well luckily for us I have something in mind myself. There's an iron grate at the back of this cell that looks like it leads into the sewers. If I had some sulphuric acid, I could melt through it and escape.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But where do we get the sulphuric acid?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Thankfully it's pretty much essential in my line of work. I have some at my home, opposite my parents' house. You'll need to get it from my cabinet and bring it here. It's the thick clear liquid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay Elena, it's me again.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Ah [player name], thank goodness. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Go to the cabinet in my home, opposite my parents' house. Find some sulphuric acid and bring it back here. It's the thick clear liquid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now what did Elena say it looked like?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay Elena, it's me again.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Ah [player name], thank goodness. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this what you needed?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: No that's not right at all. You need some sulphuric acid. It's the thick clear liquid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I'll try again.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Yes that's it! Hand it over please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Right, probably best you're not here when I escape. You should head back to my father.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. Good luck Elena. I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Now's not the time for games [player name]!

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Hello there friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Edmond. Do you know where Elena is?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: She's had to go into hiding to avoid recapture. She's hiding out at the Rehnison household in West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Edmond. I'll go and see how she's doing.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Hello [player name]. I'm afraid I can't talk right now. There's an issue with an escaped prisoner that I need to deal with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news Edmond, Elena is free.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I know, she already came by to let us know. Thank you so much for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is she now?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: She's had to go into hiding to avoid recapture. She asked me to pass on the location though. It's the Rehnison household in West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Edmond. I'll go and see how she's doing.

lines.speaker: Milli Rehnison
lines.text: Elena's come to visit! Elena's come to visit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard. Do you know where she is?

lines.speaker: Milli Rehnison
lines.text: She said she had some top secret work to do. Mummy and daddy will know.

sections.title: Talking to Martha or Ted Rehnison

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: Good day friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm looking for Elena.

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: Ah yes she said you'd be coming. They're all downstairs. The trapdoor is hidden under the bed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They?

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: You mean you don't know? The resistance of course! You'd better head down, Elena will fill you in.

sections.title: Talking to Martha or Ted Rehnison again

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Good day friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Could you remind me where Elena is?

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: She's downstairs. The trapdoor is hidden under the bed.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena! It's good to see you safe.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: And you [player name]. Thank you so much for helping to get me out of there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I couldn't just leave you to rot. I care about you, Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I care about you as well, [player name]. Thank you for not forgetting about me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all in a day's work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's going on Elena? Why are all these people here?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: This, [player name], is the Ardougne Resistance!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Ardougne Resistance?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Yes! It all started when you told me the truth about the king. I realised that it would take more than just us two to overthrow him, so I started to find allies for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, when were you planning on telling me?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I knew you were busy infiltrating the mourners so I didn't want to trouble you until we were all set up. We're just about there now though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is this how you ended up in that prison?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: It is. It happened while I was trying to gather more support for us. I was trying to convince some people of the truth behind the plague, but they didn't believe me. They reported me and I was arrested not long after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you've clearly been quite busy. I've also made some progress since we last spoke.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: With the mourners? What did you find out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The mourners have been searching for a temple under West Ardougne. There's an altar there that they want to use to summon the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: That doesn't sound good at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I restored the safeguards on the temple so they shouldn't be able to get in.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Ah, that's good to hear. Do you think we're safe now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For now, but we can't rule out King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth finding another way.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well we can't do anything about Lord Iorwerth, your elf friends will have to sort him out. But if we get our way, Lathas might not be king for much longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's your plan?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You've met Councillor Halgrive before, right? He's been helping us put a plan together.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Good to see you [player name]. Allow me to run you through what we have so far.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: So unluckily for us, the king has total control over the Paladins and the Knights of Ardougne. With them, he has a large army at his disposal, one we cannot hope to stand against.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're a councillor, can't you do anything about that?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: The council serves the king I'm afraid, we can't overrule his decisions. Nor can we take power from him. What we can do however, is step in if the king becomes unfit for rule.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the king is a traitor, surely that's enough reason.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: It is, but right now it's our word against his. And as you've already seen with Elena, he is keen to keep us silent.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Before I was arrested, we'd hoped to gather enough support among the people to be able to peacefully remove the king from power. Sadly, we've not been able to convince many of them.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: It's no surprise, really. The king has created a clever lie with the plague, one that's easy for people to believe.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Councillor Halgrive has come up with an alternative way to gather support. I can't pretend I totally approve of it, but it seems to be our only option.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: We need people to see the king for what he really is. They're clearly not going to believe us when it comes to the plague, so we'll have to resort to some dirtier tactics.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: When Elena told me about how you freed her from the prison, it gave me an idea...

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: With your disguise, you can appear to be working directly for the king. Any actions you perform in that disguise will seem like they were ordered by him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see where this is going. If I upset people while dressed as a Knight of Ardougne, they'll blame the king.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: And once they are upset, they'll be far more likely to believe what we have to say.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You can probably see why I'm not a fan now. If we're to remove the king, it should mark a new start for Ardougne. But if that's all built on a lie, are we any better than the king himself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I agree, this is a dangerous path to follow.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: It is, and yet it seems to be the only path open to us. I fear the danger of inaction could harm the people far more than a few small lies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not good, but it might be our only option.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: I agree. I fear the danger of inaction could harm the people far more than a few small lies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who cares, it's for the good of the people.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: But who are we to decide that?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: There is no perfect answer here. However, I fear the danger of inaction could harm the people far more than a few small lies.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You're right, it just makes me feel uncomfortable, all of this.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: So, [player name], are you willing to help us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Excellent, let's get to work then. Wear your Knight of Ardougne disguise and use it to turn people against the king in East Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You'll want to build support in West Ardougne as well but the knights aren't allowed here. You can use your mourner disguise instead though.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: As you turn people against the king, we'll be on hand to convince them of our cause. If we aim to recruit 80 people from across both parts of Ardougne, we should have quite the army.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of things should I do?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Anything that upsets people really. I suspect some of the others here will have a few suggestions for you. Just keep an eye out for suitable opportunities.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'll keep track of how many people we've recruited, just let me know if you need an update.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'd better get started then.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to think about this.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Of course, it is a big ask. We'll be here once you have decided.

sections.title: Talking to anyone except Elena and Councillor Halgrive before agreeing to help

lines.speaker: Carla/Jethick/Kilron/Omart
lines.text: You'd better speak to Elena, she'll fill you in on what's happening here.

sections.title: Talking to Councillor Halgrive after agreeing to help

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: We need to recruit people into our resistance so that we can overthrow the king. Elena can tell you how many people we've recruited.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Your role in this is to increase hatred for the king amongst the people. That will allow us to recruit them to our cause.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of things should I do?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Disguise yourself appropriately and perform any actions that cause upset with the citizens. With your disguise, people will think you are acting on the orders of the king.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: For East Ardougne, you'll need a Knight of Ardougne disguise. For West Ardougne, you'll want a mourner disguise.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: If you need any suggestions on what to do, ask the others here and see if they have anything for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for the help.

sections.title: Talking to Elena after agreeing to help

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey. We haven't recruited anyone into the resistance yet. You should speak to the others if you need help getting started.

sections.title: Talking to Elena before recruiting enough people

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey. We've recruited [number] people into the resistance so far. We still need [number] more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there. Do you have any suggestions for this uprising of ours?

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: I bet people would get pretty angry if they had their food taken away. You could burn the food stores in West Ardougne and claim it's to protect people from the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Won't people be willing to accept that though if they think it's preventing the plague spreading?

lines.speaker: Carla
lines.text: Some will, but don't forget that it was the authorities that supplied the food in the first place. People will soon get angry if they think that not enough care is being taken with the food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahh I see, that does make sense. I wonder if I can do something similar in East Ardougne as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Jethick, got any ideas on how to increase hatred for the king?

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: An increase in taxes would definitely cause some anger, especially if it seems that the king is making personal gain. No one likes the idea of the rich getting richer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good thinking, what's the best way to spread that sort of news?

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: You could target the citizens themselves but that would be slow going. I'd recommend going for shopkeepers instead, that will impact more people in less time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks for the help.

sections.title: Getting tips from Kilron and Omart

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, do you two know of any ways I can cause anger at the king?

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: We did have an idea. With the plague, lots of people are worried for their health. We could take advantage of that.

lines.speaker: Kilron
lines.text: There's not many doctors and nurses around here. You've got Nurse Sarah in West Ardougne and Doctor Orbon in East Ardougne but that's about it.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Imagine if the king made a new law stating that all doctors and nurses in Ardougne had to retrain. They'd be out of action for months.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: People wouldn't like that at all, thanks both.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Taxes? Look at this place, do you think I can afford to pay any taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fighting the plague isn't cheap, the money needs to come from somewhere.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: As if life in this city couldn't get any worse. Where am I going to find the money?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's for you to work out. The first payment is to be collected soon.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: A knight wouldn't be telling me this, they're not allowed in West Ardougne. I don't know who you really are, but you should leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my mourner disguise before doing that.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Citizens don't decide new taxes. You should leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my mourner disguise before doing that.

sections.title: Setting the grain sacks inside the church on fire

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: What are you doing? The people need that food!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but we have reason to believe the food is contaminated. This is for the good of everyone.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: But people will starve!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just doing my job. You should take it up with the king if you don't like it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my mourner disguise before doing that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some orders from the king for you.

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: Orders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. The king is concerned about the plague spreading into East Ardougne. He has ordered that all doctors and nurses retrain to ensure they are capable of dealing with the plague.

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: But I don't have time to retrain! The people here need me! Who will help them if I'm gone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure the king considered all of that before making his decision.

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: Well he may be king but he clearly doesn't have a clue about health. This could be disasterous for the city!

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: You knights aren't allowed in West Ardougne. You should leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my mourner disguise before doing that.

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: I don't take orders from citizens. Now I have work to be doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my mourner disguise before doing that.

sections.title: Setting the grain sacks outside the general store on fire

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: You're burning the food! We need that food!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We believe the food is contaminated with the plague. This is for your own safety.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: We'll starve without it though! You can't do this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I'll make sure everyone hears about this!

sections.title: Taxing Chadwell, the general store owner

lines.speaker: Chadwell
lines.text: Good day. What can I get you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Chadwell
lines.text: New taxes? What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The plague isn't easy to contain. New funds are needed to help the effort.

lines.speaker: Chadwell
lines.text: But I barely make any money here. I can't afford to pay new taxes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but the king himself gave the order. The first payment is due soon.

lines.speaker: Chadwell
lines.text: The people won't allow this to continue!

lines.speaker: Chadwell
lines.text: Hang on, I know you knights aren't allowed in West Ardougne. You're an imposter! Get out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my mourner disguise before doing that.

lines.speaker: Chadwell
lines.text: And why would a citizen be the one telling me this? Don't waste my time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my mourner disguise before doing that.

sections.title: Setting the grain sacks in the central Civic Office on fire

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: What do you think you're doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king has ordered us to burn this food. It could be contaminated with the plague.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: But that food is for the people. Without it they will starve!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, just doing my job.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: A lot of people are going to be angry about this!

lines.speaker: Silk merchant
lines.text: I buy silk. If you ever want to sell any silk, bring it here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Silk merchant
lines.text: New taxes? What new taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Taxes on all market stalls. The order comes from the king himself.

lines.speaker: Silk merchant
lines.text: Well I'll need to up my prices. A lot of people won't be happy about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Probably not, but they'll just have to live with it. These taxes are needed for the good of the city. Someone will come to collect the tax from you soon.

lines.speaker: Silk merchant
lines.text: The king wouldn't send a mourner to tell me this. Go away!

lines.speaker: Silk merchant
lines.text: The king wouldn't send a citizen to tell me this. Go away!

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: Good day [Sir/Madame]. Would you like some nice freshly baked bread? Or perhaps a nice piece of cake?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: What are you on about? New taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The plague isn't cheap to combat. The king has decreed that new taxes be introduced to help with the funding.

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: I appreciate the need to combat the plague but this is a foolish move. My food feeds a lot of people, they'll be furious to hear about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Better than getting the plague though. Your first payment is due soon, so you'd better be ready.

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: You mourners may deal with the plague, but I know you have nothing to do with taxes. Now leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: Citizens have nothing to do with taxes. Leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: Good day [Sir/Madame]. Would you like some nice freshly baked bread? Or perhaps a nice piece of cake?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: New taxes? What new taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king has ordered that new taxes be introduced to help fund the fight against the plague.

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: The food I provide helps to feed this city! People won't be happy when I have to increase my prices!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, it's needed though. The first payment is due soon, make sure you're ready.

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: Just because you mourners handle the plague doesn't mean you get to make new taxes. Go away!

lines.speaker: Baker
lines.text: Citizens don't deal with taxes. Go away!

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Silver! Silver! Best prices for buying and selling in all Kandarin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: New taxes? What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king has called for a tax on luxury items. Your stall is included.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: Well that is unacceptable! I will have to increase my prices. I've produced silver for the king himself before but don't expect me to do that again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As you wish, but you'll still need to pay. Your first instalment will be collected soon.

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: The king wouldn't send a mourner to tell me this. Clear off!

lines.speaker: Silver merchant
lines.text: The king wouldn't send a citizen to tell me this. Clear off!

lines.speaker: Gem merchant
lines.text: Here, look at my lovely gems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Gem merchant
lines.text: What new taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king has called for a tax on luxury items like the ones you sell.

lines.speaker: Gem merchant
lines.text: But the king is one of my biggest customers! I hope he realises that I'll have to increase my prices and he won't be exempt from that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure the king has good reason. I will return to collect your first payment soon.

lines.speaker: Gem merchant
lines.text: I don't think the king would send a mourner to tell me this. Go away!

lines.speaker: Gem merchant
lines.text: I don't think the king would send a citizen to tell me this. Go away!

lines.speaker: Fur trader
lines.text: Would you like to trade in fur?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Fur trader
lines.text: What new taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: By order of the king, all market stalls are to be taxed to help fund his new castle market.

lines.speaker: Fur trader
lines.text: His new castle market? So he's taxing us to help him set up a competing market? This is outrageous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well he's the king so he can do what he wants. Your first payment will be collected soon.

lines.speaker: Fur trader
lines.text: And he sent a mourner to inform us? Go away!

lines.speaker: Fur trader
lines.text: And he sent a citizen to inform us? Go away!

lines.speaker: Spice seller
lines.text: Are you interested in buying or selling spice?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Spice seller
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Taxes on all market stalls. It's to help fund a new market at the castle.

lines.speaker: Spice seller
lines.text: A new market could put me out of business! Why should I help fund it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because it's the law, refusing to pay is treason. Your first payment is due soon, so I suggest you prepare for it.

lines.speaker: Spice seller
lines.text: I will make sure the people hear about this!

lines.speaker: Spice seller
lines.text: A mourner wouldn't be sent to tell me this. Go away!

lines.speaker: Spice seller
lines.text: A citizen wouldn't be sent to tell me this. Go away!

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Hear ye! Hear ye! Player Moderators massive help to RuneSc-

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Oh, hello citizen. Are you here to find out about Player Moderators? Or perhaps would you like to know about the laws of the land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you help me tell people about some new taxes?

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: New taxes? You know that people won't be happy about that, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, it's the king's orders though. The castle is in need of repairs and more funds are required.

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: As you wish, I will make sure the people know. Just know that I'll be sending them right to the king if they get angry.

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: New taxes? I know that mourners don't have anything to do with taxes. Please allow me to get on with my work.

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: New taxes? A citizen wouldn't have anything to do with taxes. Please allow me to get on with my work.

sections.title: Taxing Zenesha, the armour store owner

lines.speaker: Zenesha
lines.text: Hello there! I sell plate mail bodies, are you interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Zenesha
lines.text: I'm already paying my taxes though?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These are new ones. They are to help pay for the king's upcoming visit to Great Kourend.

lines.speaker: Zenesha
lines.text: Well I don't see why I have to pay for that! If the king can't afford the trip, then maybe he shouldn't be going!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's an important diplomatic trip I'm afraid so it has to go ahead. The first payment will be collected soon.

lines.speaker: Zenesha
lines.text: And he's getting a mourner to tell me this? Nice try.

lines.speaker: Zenesha
lines.text: And he's getting a citizen to tell me this? Nice try.

lines.speaker: Estate agent
lines.text: Hello. Welcome to the Gielinor Housing Agency! What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Estate agent
lines.text: But the Gielinor Housing Agency doesn't pay taxes here! It's part of our business agreement with the king!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These taxes are not included in that agreement. They're new emergency taxes related to the plague.

lines.speaker: Estate agent
lines.text: Well this is just no good. If the king does not intend to honour his arrangement with us, we may well take our business elsewhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can do that, but while you're in Ardougne you must pay the tax. The first payment will be due soon.

lines.speaker: Estate agent
lines.text: And a mourner is the one telling me this. I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Estate agent
lines.text: And a citizen is the one telling me this. I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Probita
lines.text: Welcome to the pet insurance bureau. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Probita
lines.text: What are you on about? I already pay my taxes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king has ordered some new taxes to help fund his extensive collection of gnomish ornaments. Everyone has to contribute.

lines.speaker: Probita
lines.text: Gnomish ornaments? Why can't the king pay for his own ornaments? He's got enough money as it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The law has already been passed so it's not up for debate. Your first payment is expected soon.

lines.speaker: Probita
lines.text: I'll make sure people hear about this!

lines.speaker: Probita
lines.text: And why would he send a mourner to tell me this? Stop lying and get out!

lines.speaker: Probita
lines.text: And why would he send some random citizen to tell me this? Stop lying and get out!

sections.title: Taxing Aemad or Kortan, the general store owners

lines.speaker: Aemad/Kortan
lines.text: Hello there. You've come to the right place if you're looking for adventurer's equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Aemad/Kortan
lines.text: New taxes? What new taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The castle is in dire need of repairs, the taxes are to help fund that. They've been ordered by the king himself.

lines.speaker: Aemad/Kortan
lines.text: Ordered by the king? This is preposterous! We'll have to increase our prices to afford this, people will be furious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but rules are rules. The first payment is due soon so make sure you're ready.

lines.speaker: Aemad/Kortan
lines.text: Ordered by the king? What a load of rubbish! You mourners seem to think you own the place. Well you don't! Now clear off!

lines.speaker: Aemad/Kortan
lines.text: Ordered by the king? What a load of rubbish! I can see you're not one of his people. Stop fooling around and get lost!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Peace be with you, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Taxes? But this is a holy church of Saradomin, we are exempt from taxes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: More funding is needed to hold back the plague. The church needs to help as well.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: We'll need to use the donations from the congregation. We were hoping to use that money to build an orphanage for those who lost their parents to the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but the king has given the order. The first payment is due soon.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: It is not the job of a mourner to deal with taxes. Now, if there's nothing else, I must get to work.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: It is not the job of a citizen to deal with taxes. Now, if there's nothing else, I must get to work.

lines.speaker: Doctor Orbon
lines.text: Hello there. Can't stop to talk I'm afraid, I'm very busy right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some orders from the king for you.

lines.speaker: Doctor Orbon
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king is concerned about the plague spreading. He has ordered that all doctors and nurses in the city retrain to ensure they are capable of dealing with the plague.

lines.speaker: Doctor Orbon
lines.text: Retrain? But that will take months! Who will help the people if there are no doctors around? This is a foolish move!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the king's word is law so there's little point arguing.

lines.speaker: Doctor Orbon
lines.text: Well don't blame me when there's no one to help the king if he falls ill!

lines.speaker: Doctor Orbon
lines.text: I don't take orders from citizens. Now I really must get to work.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Would you like to buy a drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Taxes? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: By order of the king, all establishments such as this must now pay an extra beer tax.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: Beer tax? My patrons work hard for the city of Ardougne! They come here to unwind after a long day. This is plain disrespect to the work they do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's also plain disrespect to defy the king's laws so you'd better be ready to pay up. We'll be back to collect soon.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: You haven't heard the last of this!

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: And why would a mourner be telling me this? Clear off!

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: You don't represent the king! Clear off!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: New taxes? What for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king has ordered an increase in taxes on all farms in the area. It's due to a lack of care when it comes to the plague.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Lack of care? This is about Farmer Brumty and his sheep isn't it? That has nothing to do with us here! Why should we pay for his mistakes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, it's the word of the king so you'll have to pay up. The first instalment will be collected soon.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: You mourners may serve the king, but you don't have the power to enforce that. Go away!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: You don't have the power to enforce that. Go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to tell you about some new taxes.

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: New taxes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, they've been ordered by the king to help him buy a new throne.

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: No problem. However, we first need to tax you for your bank usage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... what?

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: Well it's only fair right? You tax us so we tax you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On second thoughts... forget I said anything.

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: Mourners don't deal with taxes. Nice try.

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: Citizens don't deal with taxes. Nice try.

sections.title: Once enough people have been recruited

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That should be enough people recruited, I'd better go and let Elena know.

sections.title: Talking to anyone except Elena and Councillor Halgrive

lines.speaker: Carla/Jethick/Kilron/Omart
lines.text: Good work out there. You'd better speak to Elena and Councillor Halgrive, they'll go over the next steps with you.

sections.title: Talking to Elena or Councillor Halgrive

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Welcome back [player name], and good work out there. Thanks to you we now have a force that can stand up to Lathas and his armies.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Now comes the difficult part though. Before we move against the king, we need to deal with his allies, the mourners.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do we do that?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: We now significantly outnumber the mourners, we should be able to drive them out of the city. The main focus will be their headquarters. Without their leadership, they'll soon retreat.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: We have the numbers, but the mourners are better trained and better equipped. You should make sure you're ready before we begin. There will be bloodshed, on both sides.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure you're okay with this Elena?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I don't like it, but I know it's the only way. I appreciate the concern though, thank you friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's necessary bloodshed though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: It is, it's just a shame there isn't another way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A bit of blood doesn't scare me.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: No... But you're not the one I'm worried about.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Are you ready to begin [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I will send word around to make sure our people are prepared. Good luck everyone.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: This is it! The plague of West Ardougne has gone on for long enough, today is the day of plague's end!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to prepare.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Very well, return to us once you are ready.

sections.title: After killing 10 mourners on the ground level

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Good work! Let's head downstairs and deal with the rest of them!

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: You! I knew you were trouble! Well don't worry, Lord Iorwerth will learn of your betrayal!

sections.title: After killing 11 mourners in the basement

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: We did it! Now we'd better get out of here!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Excellent work with those mourners. I suspect the king might be getting a little worried now.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: We'll need to move fast. He's probably already planning his retaliation.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: He'll likely expect us to take some time to recuperate after that battle. He won't expect us to hit him right away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So let me guess, that's exactly what we're going to do?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Indeed, we hit the castle now before they have a proper chance to prepare for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Even if they're not prepared, the castle will still be well defended.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Yes, but thanks to our victory in West Ardougne, some of the knights are starting to question their loyalties. I've taken advantage of this and made arrangements to ensure the gates are open to us.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Make no mistake, it will still be a tough fight, but it's a fight we should be able to win.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: We can afford to take a little bit of time to ensure everyone has resupplied so feel free to come back later if you're not ready yet. Just don't take too long.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Let me know when you are ready to begin [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I never thought this day would come. We're finally going to deal with that traitor once and for all!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Let's show him what it means to cross the people of Ardougne!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to prepare.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Very well, return to us once you are ready.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Look's like that's the last of them! Good work everyone!

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Look who we found hiding in a wardrobe.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: So you're a coward as well as a traitor! What a pathetic excuse of a man you are.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: You call me a traitor? I am your king! The traitors here are the ones standing before me.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Give it a rest, it's over. We know the truth.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: And what truth might that be?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: You let an enemy into our city and covered it up by lying to your people.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: And I'm sure you'd never do something like that. Don't think I didn't hear about the lies you spread about me.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: There's also your brother. Murdered on your orders.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: You are aware that his killer stands with you, are you not?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name] is innocent in this. You manipulated them for your own gain.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Oh don't you go pretending they're innocent! They're nothing more than a mercenary. All they care about is the big pot of gold at the end.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: They betrayed me! Before long, they'll betray you as well!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Liar!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Enough! You're finished Lathas. The people will decide your fate. I don't expect them to be kind.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: [player name], do you have any questions for the king before we take him away? I suspect you won't get another chance to ask them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why were you helping Lord Iorwerth?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: My father, King Ulthas, once ruled over a united Kandarin. I was his eldest son, the kingdom was my birthright! But my fool of a father took that from me.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: When King Arthur and his knights arrived in our lands, my father granted them the castle of Camelot, ousting the loyal subjects that previously lived there.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Not only that, but he also declared that the kingdom be jointly ruled by my brother and I, rather than giving it to me as he should have.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: When I ascended to my half-throne, I promised myself that I would unite this kingdom again, no matter the cost.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: That's why I made the deal with Lord Iorwerth. In return for access to West Ardougne, he agreed to help me kill my brother and drive out King Arthur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you know what the mourners were doing?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: In West Ardougne? They were searching for some sort of temple. According to Lord Iorwerth, the power there would be essential in summoning Zamorak, the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you knowingly allied with a group of Zamorakians?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Of course I did! I prayed to Saradomin that he would help unite my kingdom again. He chose not to reply. I turned to the Dark Lord instead.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: With Zamorak at my side, nothing would stand between me and Camelot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you kill your brother?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Last I checked it was you that killed him, not me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know what I mean.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: My brother was never meant to rule. The kingdom was mine by birthright! My father chose to give him half the kingdom, and he did a terrible job ruling it.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Have you seen West Ardougne? The city is a mess, and not because of the plague. That was my brother's doing. I may be a bad person, but make no mistake, my brother was no hero either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're done here, take him away.

sections.title: Talking to Councillor Halgrive after Lathas' death

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Well hello again adventurer! How are you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine thank you. And yourself?

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Not too bad, I've a lot to do though. King Lathas left plenty of things for us to clean up.

sections.title: Returning to Ardougne Castle after Lathas' death

lines.speaker: King Thoros
lines.text: On behalf of everyone in Ardougne, I thank you for your help adventurer. I'm afraid I don't have time to talk, my predecessor has left me with a lot of work to do.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Bring forth the condemned!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Citizens of Ardougne! Before you stands Lathas Ardignas: a traitor, a murderer and a liar!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: He has been found guilty of numerous heinous crimes, and will now face judgement for his sins!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So this is it. After all this time, Lathas is finally going to pay for what he's done. It's like I should feel something, but I just feel empty.

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Lathas Ardignas, as decided by the people of Ardougne, you are hereby sentenced to death!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: Do you have any final words?

lines.speaker: Lathas
lines.text: How dare you insult me like this! You can't kill me! I am the rightful king! Every person here is under my rule!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: The people have passed their judgement, you are not a king any more.

lines.speaker: Lathas
lines.text: Then you are all traitors, and you will all pay with your lives!

lines.speaker: Councillor Halgrive
lines.text: So be it...

lines.speaker: Lathas
lines.text: In the name of Zamorak I curse you, each and every one of you!

sections.title: Face to face with Lord Iorwerth

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: So you finally decided to show your true colours, how disappointing.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: We should have killed them both when we had the chance.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Hmm, perhaps.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: You know, after you killed King Tyras, I thought you might have some potential. Given time, we might have properly welcomed you into our ranks. You'd have been the first human among us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm glad to have disappointed you. I don't work with murderers.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: No? You seemed more than happy to kill King Tyras when it suited you. Burnt him alive in the middle of his own camp if I recall.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name] is innocent! You lied to them!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Lied? I told your friend here that King Tyras was an invader into our lands. Last time I checked, that was the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe, but you conveniently missed out the part about you trying to summon the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Ah, I see you have been led astray. You think we are the evil ones, but our cause is the true one. You think we are murderers, but we are in fact saviours.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: You think you're the hero of this story, but you're not...

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You're mad!

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Let me deal with them, my lord. They've caused us enough trouble.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: They may still be of use to us. Lock them away for now. Give them time to think on their choices.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Hmm, as you wish.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's enough Lord Iorwerth, let them go!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Brave of you to come here Arianwyn, but foolish. We have you outnumbered.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Don't be so sure. We fight with the strength of ten of your warriors.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Perhaps, but I don't believe you're the type to throw lives away without need. Instead, why don't we discuss this like reasonable people.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: We don't have anything to discuss with you, you Zamorakian scum!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Zamorakian? You don't really think we'd support that monster do you? I'm afraid to say that you're quite mistaken.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Don't try to manipulate us with your lies. We know all about your plot with King Lathas to summon the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Ah, that. Well now that King Lathas is dead, I suppose there's no better time to reveal the truth.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: It was just a ruse I'm afraid. A lie we told King Lathas to get him on our side.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: We needed access to West Ardougne so we needed the support of the king. Once we found out about his hidden worship of Zamorak, it seemed like an obvious lie to tell.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I have no more love for Zamorak than you do. I've only ever served one god, the same one as you, Seren.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: You're lying! You wanted to use the Temple of Light to summon Zamorak!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I can see why you'd think that, but you are mistaken. You're right to say that the temple is key to summoning a god, but that god is not Zamorak, it's Seren.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's impossible! Seren is dead!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I thought the same once. That was until I managed to break one of the seals within the Grand Library.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: That's how I discovered the truth. A part of Seren lives on, trapped within the Temple of Light.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I don't believe you!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: A shame. You're better than this Arianwyn, I hope in time you will see that.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: What should we do with them, my lord.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Let them go. Let it be known that the Iorwerth clan is not without mercy.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Are you sure about this?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Yes. But make no mistake Arianwyn, if you and your rebels interfere with our affairs again, I will not be so forgiving.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Now, I suggest you leave, before I change my mind.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: This isn't over!

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: This is a bad idea.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Don't be so sure. They may not know it yet, but the rebels could be of great use to us...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Ysgawyn?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I am, what of it human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn has called a meeting. He asked me to come fetch you.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I see, so now I'm meant to follow the orders of a human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't an order, it was just...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Make no mistake human, you may have Arianwyn and the others fooled but I know what you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know what you mean...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You're a menace. You claim you're here to help, but everywhere you go you leave a trail of bodies behind.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Anyway, I'll come to this meeting, I'm in no place to deny Arianwyn. Just stay out of my way from here on out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. Before you go, do you know where Elena is?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: The other human? I think she's by the pond to the south west. Now if you'll excuse me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... he was nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how are you doing, Elena?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'm okay. So much has happened so fast, it's a lot to take in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is. I'm here for you if you need me though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Thanks, that means a lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll get used to it.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I guess... I just don't know if I want to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, it's no small thing.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Anyway, I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: The people of Ardougne, the elves here, you've spent a lot of time helping them. Why? What drives you, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I care about helping people.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So even if you knew that you'd get nothing out of it yourself, you'd still continue to help these elves?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I suspect not many people would feel that way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm not like other people.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Clearly. Thank you for answering, I realise it's a pretty pointless question. I was curious to know how you felt though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well a reward's always nice, but yes.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I guess it's always nice to be rewarded. It's good to know that isn't all that matters to you though. I suspect not many people would feel that way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm not like other people.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Clearly. Thank you for answering, I realise it's a pretty pointless question. I was curious to know how you felt though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I'd want a reward.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I guess it's always nice to be rewarded. You should be careful not to let that drive you though. Money can't buy everything, [player name].

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I've rambled on enough. Was there something you needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn has called a meeting. He'd like you to attend.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'd better head over there then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: People reward me for helping them.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So if you didn't think you'd get a reward, you'd stop helping these elves?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, the reward is what matters.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You should be careful of letting that drive you. Money can't buy everything, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I'd still help.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So it's not just about the reward after all. It's nice to know that isn't all that matters to you. I suspect not many people would feel that way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm not like other people.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Clearly. Thank you for answering, I realise it's a pretty pointless question. I was curious to know how you felt though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I've rambled on enough. Was there something you needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn has called a meeting. He'd like you to attend.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'd better head over there then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to fix my mistakes.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So it's about what happened with King Tyras? Do you think you'd still help these elves if that hadn't happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So it's not just guilt that's driving you, that's good to know. What happened with King Tyras was awful but you can't take too much blame, Lathas manipulated you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't think I would.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So the only thing driving you is guilt? That doesn't seem healthy. Be careful that you don't carry the world on your shoulders, [player name]. It's not your fault that Lathas manipulated you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hmmm, not something you've had to think about I guess. Just make sure that you don't carry the world on your shoulders, [player name]. It's not your fault that Lathas manipulated you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, thanks Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Anyway, I've rambled on enough. Was there something you needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn has called a meeting. He'd like you to attend.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'd better head over there then. Thanks for the talk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's my duty to help people.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Be careful of hiding behind duty, it won't stop people holding you accountable for your actions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hmmm, not something you've had to think about I guess. It's worth considering though. We should all have something to drive us.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Anyway, was there something you needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn has called a meeting. He'd like you to attend.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'd better head over there then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe later.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Fair enough. Was there something you needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn has called a meeting. He'd like you to attend.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'd better head over there then.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Ah [player name], there you are. We thought it best to let you rest for a bit after everything that happened earlier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Arianwyn, I definitely needed it. And thank you for saving us back there, I don't know what we'd have done if you hadn't shown up.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I suspect you wouldn't have been around to have this conversation. You played a dangerous game in Ardougne. I fear you may not have considered the outcome of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: People were dying Arianwyn, we couldn't do nothing.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Of course not, but we have no idea how Lord Iorwerth will respond to this. The things he said when we freed you were... concerning...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can say that again. What do you think he meant by it all?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'm not sure. It's something that we should discuss with the others.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Could you do me a favour and fetch Ysgawyn. He's probably on the upper platforms. You should bring your friend Elena as well. I'm not sure where she is I'm afraid, Ysgawyn might know though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. You should talk to Arianwyn when you're ready to begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I wanted to ask you...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: No. Go away and talk to Arianwyn instead.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Hey there. You should let Arianwyn know once you're ready to get started.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Hey there [player name]. Arianwyn's leading this meeting so you should let him know when you're ready to start.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Good to see you [player name]. You should speak to Arianwyn when you're ready to begin.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Right, now that we're all here, let's get started.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: So, as I'm sure you've all heard, King Lathas of Ardougne is dead. He was overthrown by his subjects and executed soon afterwards.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's not all though, the mourners of West Ardougne have been driven out of the city. Lord Iorwerth no longer has any presence in Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Let's not ignore the obvious, Arianwyn. Our supposed ally, [player name], helped lead the uprising. His actions may well have endangered us all.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: But surely this is a good thing? With Ardougne lost to him, Lord Iorwerth no longer has access to the Temple of Light. Without that, he has no way to summon the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: The temple was already secure. All this uprising has achieved is to anger the enemy.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: You say that like it's a bad thing, Ysgawyn. Last time I checked, we didn't care about upsetting our foe.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I care about keeping our people alive, Islwyn!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: And what about our people? While you sat here in your secure little village, we took action! We saved lives! But I guess you're too selfish to care about us.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Selfish? How dare you! You know nothing of the plight of the elves, girl. You'd do well to keep your mouth shut!

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's enough Ysgawyn. We can debate what's happened for days and still reach no conclusion. That is not why I called you all here. Instead, we need to plan what happens next.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Now, while freeing [player name] and Elena from the Iorwerth camp, we were confronted by Lord Iorwerth. He could have killed us all, but he let us go.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: He let you go? Why?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: His reasoning was disturbing. For a long time now, we've believed that Lord Iorwerth has been trying to summon Zamorak, the Dark Lord. However, he claimed otherwise.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: But if not Zamorak, then who?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Seren.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Seren? But that's impossible, Seren is gone.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: He seems to think otherwise. He claimed that a part of Seren still lives, trapped within the Temple of Light. Apparently, he discovered this by breaking one of the nine seals within the Grand Library.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: That's impossible. He must be lying.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Sorry, but I don't understand what you mean by all of this. Who's Seren? What library?

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Seren was our goddess, our guardian and our teacher. Everything we have, we owe to her. She helped us build the great city of Prifddinas, a place where all elves could live in harmony.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
