lines.text: At the end of the God Wars, the gods were banished from this world. Seren didn't want to leave us though, she couldn't. She shattered herself into thousands of crystals. Those crystals are all we have left of her now.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I see, I'm sorry to hear that.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: As for the Grand Library, it was a great archive of knowledge beneath Prifddinas. Pretty much everything there was open to all of elven kind.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: There was an exception to that though, the nine seals of Seren. The seals guarded the forbidden knowledge, that which only Seren herself could access.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Lord Iorwerth managed to break one of these seals.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: So he claims...

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Is there no way we can check?

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: No. Prifddinas was lost to us. When Lord Iorwerth first took control of the city, my grandfather, Baxtorian, led an army to try and stop him.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: When that failed, Baxtorian and the other clan elders made one final ditch attempt to save Prifddinas. They sung the song of inversion to revert the entire city to a crystal seed.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Their plan worked, and the city was lost to Lord Iorwerth. However, it was lost to the rest of us as well, the Grand Library with it.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What happened to Baxtorian?

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: While he was fighting Lord Iorwerth, invaders took control of our kingdom to the east. They kidnapped Glarial, Baxtorian's wife.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Unable to find her, he sealed himself beneath the waterfall where they lived, never to be seen again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Baxtorian was king of the elves back then. Do you think he had access to the forbidden knowledge?

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: It's not impossible I suppose. If anyone, it would have been him. Baxtorian was closer to Seren than any other elf, it's one of the reasons he was chosen to become king.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Baxtorian is gone though, even if he knew something, he can't help us now.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Don't be so sure. I never knew my grandfather, but my mother always told me that he wasn't the type for taking chances. If he knew something important, he'd have made sure it wouldn't have been lost with him.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: We can't possibly make a plan without knowing Lord Iorwerth's intentions. While unlikely to be true, this stuff about the library is all we have to go on.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: If Baxtorian left behind anything that could help us, we should try to find it.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Baxtorian kept everything of importance to him at his home within Baxtorian Falls. If we're going to find anything, it's probably there.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: [player name], you've been to Baxtorian Falls multiple times. You're the best person to take the lead on this.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You trust the human with this?

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Let go of your pride, Ysgawyn. [player name] has proven his worth to us.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'd like to accompany you on this one [player name], it might be useful to have an elf with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Before her death, my mother passed down a book to me. It was written by my grandfather for his wife. You should take it with you. It might contain something of use.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Thank you Islwyn. While we're gone, the rest of you should focus on Lord Iorwerth. See if you can get any idea on what he's planning now that he's lost Ardougne. Just be careful.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: We always are. Good luck to you both.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: And you. Let's get started.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Thank you for agreeing to help us, [player name]. We owe a lot to you, even if some of us don't show it very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean Ysgawyn? He is a bit rude.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Try not to be too judgemental. Ysgawyn's husband was killed by Essyllt, one of Lord Iorwerth's henchmen. Ysgawyn's never really been the same since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met Essyllt, he was the one in charge of the mourners in West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: He's a nasty piece of work. If you try hard enough, you can find something redeemable about most elves, even those that serve Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: But Essyllt... He seems to take joy in the suffering of others, especially those unable to defend themselves.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Enough about him though, we need to focus on Baxtorian Falls. I suggest we travel seperately, we can't be too careful at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Makes sense, will you be okay getting there on your own?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'm no expert on your lands but I should be fine. I just have a few things to gather before I depart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well in that case, I'll meet you there.

sections.title: Talking to Arianwyn after the meeting

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I just have a few things to gather before I depart for Baxtorian Falls. I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good, see you there.

sections.title: Talking to Elena after the meeting

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Good to see you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things going?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Pretty good. I've been thinking about that elf, Ysgawyn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bit of an angry guy isn't he?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: That's one way to put it. I can't say I approve of his attitude, but I appreciate he's seen a lot in this war.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: It's interesting, the elves have such long lifespans, we're just children compared to them. And yet, even with all their experience, they're still just as fickle as we are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I find most races are the same to be honest.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Anyway, had any luck with Baxtorian Falls?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'll let you crack on then. We'll talk more later.

sections.title: Talking to Ysgawyn after the meeting

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I wanted to ask you...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: No. Go away.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Hello there [player name], glad to see you made it here okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You as well Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I must say, it feels strange to be back here, especially after all of this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been here before?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: A long time ago...

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I was one of the elves born in Baxtorian's new kingdom. My family were close relations of Baxtorian, he invited us here a few times.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Those were simpler times, happier times. After Baxtorian left to try and retake Prifddinas, my parents were among those who joined him. They never returned. I was still just a child at the time, alone and afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: This war has taken so much... From my people, and from yours. I'd give anything to see peace, anything to finally see Prifddinas with my own eyes.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Sorry, now's not the time for this. We need to see if Baxtorian left anything of use behind.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Here's that book that Islwyn gave us. Hopefully it has something of use in it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you believe what Lord Iorwerth said about Seren?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: To be honest... I really don't know. It seems impossible, and yet...

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: No. It's just me being wishful. He's lying, he has to be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wishful?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: By the time I was born, Seren was already gone from this world. All I know of her is the stories my parents told me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You wish you could have met her?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I do, but that doesn't mean I should fall for the lies of Lord Iorwerth. Seren is gone, nothing can ever bring her back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think we'll find anything to help us here?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I won't lie, we're clutching straws here. We have nothing else to go on though, so we have to try.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Baxtorian was once king of the elves, anything he left behind might be of use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll crack on.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: We need to see if Baxtorian left anything of use behind. Maybe that book will be of use.

sections.title: Placing the items on the respective pillars

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Glarial! I... oh...

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [player name] and this is Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Arianwyn... I remember that name. You grew up in my kingdom... I'm sorry about your family.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Don't be your majesty...

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I appreciate the sentiment, but please, I am no king anymore.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Now I must ask... Glarial... Is there any news?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'm sorry but... she's gone.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... I see... It was always a lot to hope for...

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: If it's any consolation, [player name] moved her remains here, to be closer to you.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: That... That means a lot. Thank you. It is good to see that some humans still see elves as friends.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: If you need time...

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: No... I feel I've spent long enough here.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Then I can take you back to Lletya. Your grandson, Islwyn, is there.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Grandson? So Eirlys had a child? Does she still live as well?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'm afraid not... I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: No father should outlive their child. Forgive me Eirlys.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Please, let us leave now. There is too much sadness for me here, too much pain. Once we arrive, you must tell me everything that has happened in my absence.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Very well, I will teleport us back to Lletya. You'll have to make your own way back I'm afraid, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. See you soon.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello there. You'd probably best talk to Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to ask you something about yourself.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I see... Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you seal yourself away?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: My Glarial... She mattered more to me than anything else, but she was lost to me. The enemy took her, so I searched far and wide to find her again.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: At times I came close, but in the end, I was too weak to carry on. I knew there was a chance she may live though, so I sealed myself away, hoping she would be the one to wake me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I thank you friend, but please, don't be. The burden is mine to bear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened between you and Lord Iorwerth?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: To be honest, even today I know little of him. When I left Prifddinas to expand our kingdom into the east, he was but a child, with the Iorwerth clan ruled by his mother.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The first time I properly met him was after I heard about his takeover of the city. I led my forces through the Underground Pass and confronted him at the city gates.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I tried to reason with him, but he had already decided that I was his enemy. We had the numbers and our forces were better skilled, so we tried to besiege the city.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The Iorwerth forces had the strength and safety of the city walls though. They slowly whittled us down until we were too weak to carry on. We had no choice but to retreat.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: We fell back into the Underground Pass, intending to return to our kingdom in the east. Lord Iorwerth sent his forces in after us, hoping to wipe us out.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: We no longer had the strength in numbers, but we still outmatched the Iorwerth troops in skill and experience. We fought back while we retreated. Both sides suffered huge losses.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Eventually we managed to make it through to the other side. By then, there were few of us left. Lord Iorwerth had failed to wipe us out, but he knew we were beaten.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: While we were gone, an uprising had occurred in the eastern kingdom. We took our best fighters to Prifddinas, those that remained were unable to hold back the enemy.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: My wife was taken and most of my people were slaughtered. I turned my attention to finding my wife and getting the rest of my people to safety.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth became the least of my problems. I suspect he didn't consider me much of a threat at that point anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was Seren like?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: She... She was wonderful. She taught us to better ourselves in every way possible. Everything we know, everything we are, it all came from her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish I could have met her.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I feel very blessed to have been able to. The love she had for us, it's unlike anything I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like she was pretty much perfect.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Well... not quite. Many of the elves alive now were not around when Seren was still with us. They only know her through the stories passed down to them. The stories don't tell everything though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Seren was remarkable, but under the surface, she hid a great pain. A sadness unlike any other. She never spoke of it, but when you looked hard enough, you could see the anguish.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I guess we all have our demons, even the gods. Seren was a beacon of light in this grim world, but the brightest light casts the darkest shadow, and none shone brighter than her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of the current elves?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Arianwyn and the rebels have done well to keep going for this long. They've been fighting this war for centuries, and yet they've never given up. I have much respect for them, they're far stronger than I ever was.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Most of them are still young though. They lack the wisdom of the older elves, the ones who were around when Seren still walked this earth. Many of them never saw Prifddinas before the song of inversion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that a bad thing?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Not necessarily, but it is something to be cautious of. That which they fight for, it's a mere dream to them. They should remember that dreams seldom come true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better be off.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I've given Elena the information she needs to make that potion. You should speak to her by the pond.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Baxtorian has given Elena the information she needs to make this potion. You should speak to her by the pond.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Welcome back [player name]. While you were gone, I filled Baxtorian in on everything that has happened. I also took the liberty of calling the others here, so that we can discuss our next move.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: You also missed out on Islwyn getting to meet his grandfather for the first time. I think he might have cried a little.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Cried? In your dreams Eluned.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I appreciate we're all happy to have Baxtorian back with us, but let's not get distracted.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Apologies, Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Right, over to you Baxtorian.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Thank you, Arianwyn. I... Forgive me, it's been a long time since I last chaired a meeting like this. Much has changed since then as well.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Take your time.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Thank you friend.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Arianwyn has informed me of Lord Iorwerth's revelation...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Do you believe his claims?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: About Seren? No. As for him breaking a seal in the Grand Library... I... do not know.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You don't know? What do you mean you don't know?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Ysgawyn...

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I know not what the seals hide. What I do know is that they could, in theory, be broken.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I was lucky enough to be trusted by Seren. She confided in me many times. She never told me what knowledge the seals guard, but she did tell me that they could be destroyed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I don't know exactly. She said that those who follow the light would be able to access her knowledge.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: And that's it? This gives us nothing to go on.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Not nothing. We now know that the seals can be destroyed, which means Lord Iorwerth could have spoken the truth.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: But without access to the Grand Library, we have no way to be sure.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Well... we might still have a way.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: A way to access the library?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Yes... Prifddinas may have been reverted to seed, but it does still exist.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You're not suggesting we restore the city are you? Even if we could, Lord Iorwerth would instantly take it.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: No. While Lord Iorwerth is at large, Prifddinas must remain as it is. However, there may be a way to still enter the city.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: You don't mean entering the crystal do you?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: That's not possible.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Actually, I think it would be possible. The crystal seed that contains Prifddinas, it's not the same as the seeds we use to form weapons and armour.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: The seed contains Prifddinas, but the seed itself is not the city.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So it's like a box?

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Exactly, and if we can enter the box...

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: We can enter Prifddinas!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: That's the idea. It won't be straightforward though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it ever?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: We will need a special potion to be able to safely enter the crystal seed. We'll also need to be able to bypass the defences around it as well.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: A potion? Sounds like my area of expertise.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Perfect. Elena, if you could help create this potion, that would be greatly appreciated. The rest of us can work on those defences.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Right. [player name], meet me by the pond. I could use your help with this.

sections.title: Talking to Elena before getting a Cadantine seed

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Do you have that seed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: It's a cadantine seed that we need. Let me know once you have one. You'll want to get a seed dibber as well so we can plant it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Talking to Elena after getting a Cadantine seed

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Do you have that seed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have one here.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Great. The patch is all prepared so just plant it when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the seed planted.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Great. I'll keep an eye on the patch while it grows. You should check in with Arianwyn and Baxtorian in the meantime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, see you later.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Glad everything went okay at Baxtorian Falls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. Anything exciting happen while I was gone?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Nope, all nice and quiet for a change.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to hear.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Baxtorian is an interesting one, isn't he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He does seem a bit...

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Lost?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: He reminds me of a story my father once told me. A story of a broken man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was the story?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: A long time ago, there were two brothers living in Avarrocka. They lived a simple life, farming the land and selling the produce to people in the city.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: When war came to the city, the king ordered that every able-bodied man take up arms against the enemy. The two brothers had to leave their home behind, to fight for a king they'd never met.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Like everyone else, they were given basic weapons and basic training. Enough to give them a chance, but not a big one. When the fighting started, they were poorly equipped and poorly prepared.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: They fought in many battles. Battles in strange places against strange people. As the war dragged on, they saw more and more horrors, from both sides. The only thing that kept them going was each other.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: As the war drew to a close, they were called to one final battle. The enemy knew they were losing, and they fought more ferociously than ever before.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: That was where it happened. One of the brothers was cut off and surrounded. He tried to fight back, but he didn't stand a chance.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Eventually the fighting stopped, and the war was over. The other brother could finally go home. But the truth is, he had nothing to go home to.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: It wasn't the farm that mattered, not really. The thing that mattered to him was gone.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: In a way, he'd died back on the battlefield alongside his brother. Left in his place was just a broken man, with nothing left to live for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. And you think Baxtorian is like this broken man?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: He lost his wife, his people and his kingdom. Now he's in a strange place, surrounded by strange people, helping to fight a war that he lost a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now's not the time for stories, Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I guess you're right. The short of it is, Baxtorian has lost everything that he cared about. He's just an empty shell now, with nothing left to live for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe he's not totally lost. He still has Islwyn, and we can still turn this war around.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Maybe. I guess only time will tell.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Right. Shall we get started with this potion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we need?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Baxtorian gave me the details. To start with, we need an elder cadantine herb. We should be able to grow one from a normal cadantine seed, we just need to prepare the patch a little differently.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: The elves have said we can use this farming patch here. While I prepare the patch, could you go and fetch a cadantine seed and a seed dibber?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already have the seed here.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well it also happens that I've actually already prepared the patch, so you can get started right away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How convenient.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Indeed. When you're ready, go ahead and plant the seed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: We need you to find Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn for us. Speak to Baxtorian, he can tell you about the clues they left behind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Hello [player name], how's the potion going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We've planted the herb we need, now we have to wait for it to grow. Is there something I can do in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I suspect there is. Myself and Baxtorian have been looking over how we can get past the defences around the crystal seed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What defences are there?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The crystal seed containing Prifddinas, it lies in the middle of the clearing where the city once stood. The only thing left of the city itself are the walls.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: These are no ordinary walls though. Long ago, they were reinforced by various enchantments to protect Prifddinas during the God Wars.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: With Prifddinas gone, Lord Iorwerth has claimed the land where it once was. He's managed to manipulate the defences to only allow his allies to enter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do we get past them?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: While the Iorwerth clan may hold some magical knowledge... they are not mystics. I have no doubt that their work was crude at best, work that a true master of the magical arts could overcome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And where do we find a true master of the magical arts?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: There were eight separate elven clans that journeyed with Seren to this world. Those eight clans still exist today, with each elf born into one of them.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: You'll have already heard of some of the clans. The Iorwerth clan is known to you, the traitorous clan of assassins and slayers.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: And the Cadarn clan of course, the warrior clan of which myself and Arianwyn are part of.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: The other clans you might be less familiar with. However, one of those clans is key here, the Amlodd clan, the clan of mystics.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: It was the Amlodd clan who created the Prifddinas defences. Their clan should have the knowledge needed to overcome them. And of all the Amlodd clan, none know more than Lord Ieuan Amlodd himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And where do we find him?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: After Prifddinas was reverted to seed, the clan elders went into hiding. Before we parted ways, the others did leave me with some clues on how to find them.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Lord Amlodd may be our priority, but the other elders could be of great help in our fight against Lord Iorwerth. We should try and find them all.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I've managed to decipher the clue left behind by Lord Amlodd. We should be able to use it to find him. I've also started work on the other clues.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Apart from Lord Amlodd, I've only been able to decipher Lady Trahaearn's clue so far. I'll keep working on the others though.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: [player name], myself and Baxtorian will continue to work on deciphering these clues. While we do, it would be of great help if you could find Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find Lord Amlodd?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Here is the clue he left behind: 'The elven crystal will lead you to me.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The Amlodd clan is a clan of mystics. Some sort of magic may be key to finding Lord Amlodd. You might also find this of use, Lord Amlodd gave it to me before we went our seperate ways.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find Lady Trahaearn?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Here is the clue she left behind: 'I will journey south from the well.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The Trahaearn clan is a clan of smiths and miners. It's possible that you'll need to use your smithing and mining skills to find Lady Trahaearn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Hello again, how is it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not bad, thank you.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: It's good to have you helping us. We're all very grateful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a seed from Baxtorian that I think needs enchanting.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Okay, let me see what I can do with it.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: There you go. Be careful with it, I'm not sure what it will do when used.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the warning, see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's the least I can do. I'll see you later.

sections.title: Talking to Lord Amlodd once he is in Lletya

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: The rebels have created a wonderful village here. A safe refuge from the tyranny of Lord Iorwerth. Curious how something so beautiful could be born of something so terrible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They've done well to keep up the fight for this long.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: They have, but I'd expect no less of them. The song of the elves has been sung for thousands of years, it will continue for thousands more.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: However, I fear that this verse is the darkest yet.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Look at this place... It's beautiful, is it not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Lord Amlodd?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Amlodd? A curious name... Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Baxtorian sent me. He gave me a crystal that belonged to Lord Amlodd. It brought me here, to you.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: To me? I see...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: I have a question for you. This valley before us, how would you describe it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Beautiful.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Yes... It's nice to look upon such beauty, it reminds me of my home. The crystal spires of Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dangerous.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Yes... This world... it's so peaceful, so beautiful. But there is still danger here. It takes me back to the forests near my home, before Lord Iorwerth brought a different danger upon us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Peaceful.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Yes... It is a welcome break from the wars of my home, the wars of Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Boring.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: I suppose it can seem that way. Little to see and little to do. But there is beauty in that simplicity, much like the forest of Isafdar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: The answers are not always easy to find. I have had time to reflect here, to consider this world. Maybe you need more time to find your own answer. Alas, it is time we do not have, for Lord Iorwerth awaits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you are Lord Amlodd?

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Indeed, and if Baxtorian sent you, then it is time for me to leave this place. Tell me human, where must I travel to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The village of Lletya, in the forest of Isafdar.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Then we shall leave at once. Allow me to teleport you back with me.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Who has awoken me? A human? You are not who I expected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Baxtorian sent me. We need your help in the fight against Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Baxtorian lives? And he is working with a human? This is most irregular. I see you repaired my exoskeleton as well. Impressive work for a human, but again, most irregular.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Tell me human, where is Baxtorian?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's in the elven village of Lletya, south of here.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Then I will journey there at once. There is clearly a lot I need to catch up on.

sections.title: Talking to Lady Trahaearn once she is in Lletya

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: These rebels had better get a move on and defeat Lord Iorwerth. Old ladies like me have a tendency to drop dead, and I'll be very annoyed if that happens before I see Prifddinas again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you be annoyed if you're dead?

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Oh I'll cook up quite the storm in the afterlife, believe me.

sections.title: Talking to Arianwyn once Amlodd and Trahaearn are in Lletya

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Good job finding Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn. Now, you should probably go see how that seed you planted is doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea, I'll go see Elena.

sections.title: Talking to Baxtorian once Amlodd and Trahaearn are in Lletya

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello there. Thank you for finding Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My pleasure.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Have you checked in with Elena recently? That seed has probably grown by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go talk to her.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. The elder cadantine herb grew perfectly. Let's get it harvested.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what else do we need for the potion?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: The other ingredient is crystal dust. Hopefully you can get some from Arianwyn or Baxtorian. Once you've got some dust, just add it to a vial of water along with the herb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll go see if I can get some crystal dust.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Don't forget this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena, what do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Add the elder cadantine to a vial of water and then add some crystal dust. Hopefully either Arianwyn or Baxtorian can give you some crystal dust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some crystal dust for this potion I'm making.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Ah yes... I don't have anything you can use I'm afraid. Maybe Arianwyn can help you.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Hello [player name]. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on getting that potion made. I need some crystal dust for it though.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Crystal dust? Interesting. Well I have an old crystal here that you can use. It will need grinding up though.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Hello [player name]. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on getting that potion made. I need some crystal dust for it though.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Why don't you try using that crystal I gave you?

sections.title: Talking to Elena after finishing the potion

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena, I've finished the potion.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Great, you should take it to Arianwyn and Baxtorian.

sections.title: Talking to Baxtorian after finishing the potion

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished the potion.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... You should give it to Arianwyn then.

sections.title: Trying to drink the inversion potion before talking to Arianwyn

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably take this to Arianwyn and Baxtorian rather than drinking it.

sections.title: Talking to Arianwyn after finishing the potion

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Hello [player name]. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished the potion.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Great, I'll look after it for you. Now, let's call the others and discuss our plan for the Grand Library.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Right, thanks to [player name], we should now have everything we need to enter the crystal seed containing Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: So we have a way to bypass the defences?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Indeed we do. Baxtorian's knowledge allowed [player name] to reunite us with Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn. It was the Amlodd clan that created those defences, Lord Amlodd can overcome them for us.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: My magic can be used to teleport someone through the defences and into the crystal. It will require great focus though, I will only be able to send one of you in.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The inversion potion will ensure that whoever is sent survives the journey, now we just need to decide who that is.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: I propose we send [player name] in. They have proven to us time and time again that they have the skill needed for a challenge like this.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You cannot be serious? Even if [player name] has proven their worth as you claim, which I have my doubts about, they'll never be deemed worthy enough to unlock Seren's hidden knowledge.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Why? Because they're not an elf?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Exactly! If Seren created a way for the seals to be destroyed, she would have made it so that only an elf could break them.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Were you there when Seren made those seals, boy?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I... Well of course not.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Then how do you know what our goddess intended?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I just think...

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: I don't know this human here, but I damn well don't know you either. The human was capable enough to find me and fix my exoskeleton. Maybe they are capable of this as well.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Then I propose that [player name] be the one sent into the crystal. Anyone disagree?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Yes we already know you do Ysgawyn. Anyone else?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Right, [player name], talk to Lord Amlodd when you are ready. Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to Baxtorian after the meeting

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... Hello. You should probably talk to Lord Amlodd.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

sections.title: Talking to Arianwyn after the meeting

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Good luck in the library, [player name]. We're all counting on you. Speak to Lord Amlodd when you're ready.

sections.title: Talking to Elena after the meeting

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name] Good luck in the library. We'll talk more when you get back.

sections.title: If you exit the Library and want to go back

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Do you wish to return to the Grand Library?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do this.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Very well. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: The Grand Library of Prifddinas, such a beautiful place. You will be the first to step foot there for hundreds of years.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do once I arrive?

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: They say Lord Iorwerth broke one of the nine seals of Seren within the library. Find this seal, and see what knowledge he uncovered.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Once that is done, seek out the other eight seals, see if you can break them as well. Their knowledge may be key to our fight against the darkness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea how to break the seals?

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: According to Baxtorian, Seren herself said that those who follow the light will be able to access her knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't give me a lot to go on.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: No, but it is all you have. Now, when you are ready, I can teleport you into the crystal seed. I have the potion that you made earlier, it will ensure you survive the journey.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: One last thing, the laws that this land abide by, they may not apply in the library.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: The library has been reverted to crystal. That which is impossible out here, may well be normal in an environment like that. You should be cautious.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Now, are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do this.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Very well. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me go and prepare first.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Ithell. You'll find it on the middle level, to the south east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Ithell. You'll find it on the middle level, to the south east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Seren
lines.text: And so comes another, to seek the knowledge of Seren. Greetings mortal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You... You're Seren?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: No... I'm just a memory. A mere fragment of something far greater. A guardian of the knowledge that she left behind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek that knowledge, to help in the fight against Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: It is not my place to question why. I only exist to pass on that which she sealed away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll give me the knowledge?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seals guard the truth. This seal has been broken, its knowledge is now open to all. Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...Therein lies my guilt. A part of me, trapped and alone. Dark, yes, but still a part of my being, sealed away in the Temple of Light.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'The protections on the temple, they should keep her contained. But in case they don't, I created one last safeguard, a prison within a prison. With it I made a key, an orb of light, to unlock the darkness.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'I could have destroyed it, sealed her away forever. But I still question my decision, and so I hid the orb away, while I think upon my choices.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'The shrine at Gorlah, that is where it lies. With it, she can be freed... or destroyed...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Lord Iorwerth spoke the truth. A part of Seren lives within the Temple of Light. But why?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I have imparted the knowledge of this seal. There is nothing more here to give. If you seek the rest, then the other seals must be broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Start with the Seal of Ithell, to the south east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Cadarn. You'll find it on the bottom level, to the south. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me somewhere?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I can only take you to a seal which has already been broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Cadarn. You'll find it on the bottom level, to the south. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'When I first encountered the elves on their homeworld of Tarddiad, it was like I had finally found my purpose. I quickly fell in love with them, and they with me.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'I set out to help them, to guide them. They immediately embraced this, and one of their mystics, Ceidwad, documented my teachings in a great book called the Cerddi.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'It was the start of something beautiful, but there is pain in beauty, as I soon came to realise...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Cadarn. You'll find it on the bottom level, to the south. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Crwys. You'll find it on the middle level, to the north west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me somewhere?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I can only take you to a seal which has already been broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Crwys. You'll find it on the middle level, to the north west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...Compared to many of the mortal races I had encountered, the elves had reasonable lifespans. And yet, it wasn't long enough.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'As I spent time with the elves, I watched as they withered and died. Each death was a blade through my heart, but none more so than Ceidwad, my closest companion.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'It didn't seem fair, didn't seem right. They deserved more. I knew I had to give it to them...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Crwys. You'll find it on the middle level, to the north west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Amlodd. You'll find it on the bottom level, to the west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me somewhere?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I can only take you to a seal which has already been broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Amlodd. You'll find it on the bottom level, to the west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...I found a way to share my essence with the elves, a piece of my being for each one of them.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'With this gift, their years would number in the thousands, time enough to live the lives they deserved.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'In keeping with elven tradition, the gift was given through a song. They called it the song of creation, for it was the creation of a new life for them. At least, so we thought...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Amlodd. You'll find it on the bottom level, to the west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Meilyr. You'll find it on the top level, to the west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me somewhere?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I can only take you to a seal which has already been broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Meilyr. You'll find it on the top level, to the west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...With all my knowledge, I should have known not to meddle. That which I intended as a gift, soon turned out to be their curse.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'The song of creation increased their lifespans, but at a terrible price. It bound them to me. To stray too far from their sides, caused pain, and even death.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'I knew I had to help them, to fix my mistakes. I should have known that I'd only make it worse...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Meilyr. You'll find it on the top level, to the west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Hefin. You'll find it on the top level, to the south west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me somewhere?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I can only take you to a seal which has already been broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Hefin. You'll find it on the top level, to the south west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...Another being came to our world. Divine like myself, but not by design. He spoke of a perfect world, one filled with power.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'I knew it was a risk, but I decided to travel there. The power he spoke of might have been the only thing capable of saving the elves.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'The curse made it impossible for me to go without them, so they had to come with me, and leave their home behind. It was hard on them, but in the end, I managed to convince them...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Hefin. You'll find it on the top level, to the south west. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Trahaearn. You'll find it on the top level, to the east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me somewhere?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I can only take you to a seal which has already been broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Trahaearn. You'll find it on the top level, to the east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...We made our home in the new world, in a great city of crystal spires. It was as beautiful as the world from which we came. But beneath that beauty, the curse remained.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'The power that filled the world was even greater than I had anticipated. A higher power, one I had not encountered for a long time.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'I was sure this world held the key to breaking the curse, I just needed to find it...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Trahaearn. You'll find it on the top level, to the east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Iorwerth. You'll find it on the middle level, to the north east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you teleport me somewhere?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: I can only take you to a seal which has already been broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Iorwerth. You'll find it on the middle level, to the north east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...It took millennia, but I finally made a breakthrough. Deep beneath the mountains near our home, I found a source of deathly energy.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'I was sure I could use this power to save them. My essence is of life, of light. This darkness would be the perfect counter, a way to nullify the curse.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'I should have known not to be so foolish. This curse started with my meddling, my meddling was never going to end it...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Iorwerth. You'll find it on the middle level, to the north east. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: The seeker of knowledge returns. Do you wish to hear the knowledge again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Here is what you seek:

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you seek more of the knowledge, look to the Seal of Cadarn. You'll find it on the bottom level, to the south. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: If you wish, I can return you to the central chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me somewhere else?

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Where would you like to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: Then I have nothing more to give. If you wish to hear a different piece of knowledge, look to the other seals. The light will guide the way...

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: '...I am a being of light, but even within me, there was some darkness. I poured this darkness into my creation, combining it with the deathly energy I had found.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'When I was done, I looked upon my creation. A fragment of myself, but one of darkness, rather than light. I thought her essence would counter my own, ending the curse with her mere existence.'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: 'It was not to be though. The curse was tied to me, not to her. I had failed, and she knew it. She turned on me, tried to destroy me. I had to stop her, so I sealed her away, in the very place I had created her...'

lines.speaker: Memory of Seren
lines.text: And so ends the knowledge of this seal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Seren split off a fragment of herself, hoping it would help fix her mistakes. That's what lives within the Temple of Light, that's what Lord Iorwerth is trying to summon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should return to Lletya and let the others know what I've discovered.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Your work in the library, is it done?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: Then you should speak with Arianwyn at once.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm done with the library.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I see... You should tell Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. You'd better speak with Arianwyn if you're done in the library.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: [player name], you have returned. Did you discover anything in the Grand Library?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did. I think we should call the others.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I see. I will summon them at once.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Okay, thank you all for joining us. In case you weren't aware, [player name] here has returned from the Grand Library with some news for us.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: So what happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I entered the library and found the seal broken by Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: So he did break one of the seals. Did it hide anything notable?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it contained a memory, left behind by Seren herself.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: A memory of Seren? Amazing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was only part of something larger though. I had to break the other eight seals to uncover the rest of it.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: So you managed to break all of Seren's seals? Very impressive [player name]. Now tell us, this memory, what was it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's where it gets interesting... The memory spoke of a curse on the elves, a curse that Seren was desperately trying to break.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: A curse? What curse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A curse binding the elves to Seren. As long as she remained close, it was unnoticeable, but if she went too far... pain, and death.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: But who was responsible? Who cursed our people?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the thing... It was Seren herself.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Seren was our goddess, she would never curse us. How dare you suggest otherwise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm telling the truth!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Truth? You don't know the meaning of the word!

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Let them finish Ysgawyn!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She didn't mean to do it, she was trying to help. She was using her own essence to try and extend your lifespans, it just went wrong...

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: I... I don't believe it. She cursed us? Our goddess cursed us?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: If... If what you say is true, how does it relate to Lord Iorwerth's claims?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seren went to great lengths to undo the curse. She believed that she could nullify it by creating a being of darkness, one to counter her light essence.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: You don't mean...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth spoke the truth. She created a fragment of herself, filled with darkness, hoping to use it to break the curse. It didn't work though, so she sealed it in the Temple of Light.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: I... I don't...

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: This can't be real... This is a dream... Tell me this isn't real!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: This is a lie! It has to be! The human is showing their true colours once again!

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Boy, what did I tell you about insulting the human!

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Arianwyn? What do we do?

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Arianwyn?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Why are we still stood here? This human needs putting in chains!

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Arianwyn? Do something!

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I... I don't...

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Arianwyn!

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I don't know what to do!

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I don't know what to do...

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: You think I know something you all don't? You think I have some trick hidden up my sleeve? I have nothing!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You're our leader! Lead!

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: And how would you have me do that, Ysgawyn? How am I meant to respond to this?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You can start by locking this human up. They are a traitor! The curse is clearly a lie!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: If we were truly tied to Seren, we'd have all dropped dead when she was shattered! The curse clearly doesn't exist!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Well... Don't be so sure of that.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Nice of you to suddenly speak up! I'd have thought a king would command a bit of authority rather than crying in the corner!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'm not a king any more...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Well thank Seren for that! You only managed to lose the entire kingdom to Lord Iorwerth!

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: You should learn some respect!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I have respect! I respect the heroes who died in this war, not the coward that ran away from it!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: You... You're right. I failed you, I failed you all. I wish I could go back and change that, but I can't. All I can do now, is try and make things right.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: While horrifying, I believe [player name] speaks the truth. They have no reason to lie to us, and what they say does make sense.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Most of you never met Seren. You only know her through the stories passed down to you. But I did know her, and I know she was filled with pain.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: You... You think this is the reason?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I think it could be. As for why we don't feel the effects of the curse now, that is because Seren is still with us, every one of us.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Of course, the crystal seeds. All of us carry them in some form or other, and all of them came from Seren when she shattered herself.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Her last attempt to break the curse, after everything else failed...

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: So what do we do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's one other thing. Seren built a second set of defences within the Temple of Light, this fragment of her is sealed within them. There's an orb that can bypass these defences.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: An orb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, apparently she hid it somewhere called Gorlah.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I know of Gorlah. It's an old shrine north of Prifddinas, a place for quiet worship away from the city.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Does Lord Iorwerth know about the orb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Then he might already have claimed it. We should send someone to check.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I will go. I wish to see some actual proof of what this human claims.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: That's not your call. Arianwyn is in charge here.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Am I? My whole life, I've been fighting Lord Iorwerth. My whole life, I've believed him to be a servant of Zamorak. Now I learn that everything I fought for was a lie.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I don't even know what I'm leading any more...

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Maybe Lord Iorwerth fights for a different cause to what we thought, but that doesn't change who he is, that doesn't change what he's done.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I need time alone... Time to think... Baxtorian, I would appreciate it if you could take charge for a while.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... I... I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That's all I ask. Now, I would like to be alone.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: You're not the only one. I think we all need time to process this.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Then let us close this meeting. We will reconvene later.

sections.title: Talking to Baxtorian after the meeting

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: You should try and find Lord Crwys and Lady Hefin. They could be of big help to us in the fight against Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we do now?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: There's a lot to think about, for all of us. There's also a lot we still don't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think Arianwyn will be okay?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I hope so... I know the pain he feels, it can tear you apart if you're not careful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem to be dealing with it okay.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'm glad it seems that way, that's not how it feels though. I knew of Seren's troubles, but to know the cause... it's heart-breaking.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Now I find myself leading once more, something I swore I would never do again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn seems to think you're capable.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Yes... But Arianwyn doesn't know me, not really.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Anyway, I suppose my job now is to make sure we don't lose sight of our goal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do we know what that is anymore?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: To stop Lord Iorwerth. Even if we now know his true intentions, even if some think those intentions may be good, we can't ignore his actions.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: He'd see us all dead just to free this fragment of Seren. He has to be stopped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Ysgawyn has gone to see if he can find the orb you spoke of. No one actually gave him permission, but he seems to care little for orders at the moment.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Once he gets back we'll know more about what to do next. In the meantime, I've managed to decipher the clues left behind by Lord Crwys and Lady Hefin, two more of the clan elders. We should try to find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find Lord Crwys?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Here is the clue he left behind: 'The trees of my home will call to me.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The Crwys clan is a clan of woodcutters and farmers. The entire clan is close to nature, but none more than Lord Crwys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I find Lady Hefin?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Here is the clue she left behind: 'I will walk across the water to the south.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The Hefin clan are our religious leaders. They teach us to better ourselves, not just in mind, but in body as well. Lady Hefin used to lead some of the more vigorous training exercises of the Hefin clan.

sections.title: Talking to Lord Crwys once he is in Lletya

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: It's strange to be an elf again. I spent so long living as a tree I almost forgot what my true form felt like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe the tree was your true form and this is just your disguise.

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: Hmmm. Now there's an interesting thought...

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: Ahh... Stop, stop stop!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... You're not a tree.

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: A tree? No of course not! I am an elf, but through deep magic, I took on the form of a tree. It allowed me to hide, to stay safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safe from Lord Iorwerth?

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: You know of Lord Iorwerth?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. You must be Lord Crwys, Baxtorian sent me to find you. We need your help.

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: I see. Where is Baxtorian?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The village of Lletya. It's east of here.

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: Then it is time for me to uproot. I will head there at once.

sections.title: Talking to Lady Hefin once she is in Lletya

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: The road to enlightenment is a perilous one. The latest peril is the largest I've ever seen though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think we can overcome it.

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: That is not for me to say. All I can do is see where this path takes me. And if this is the final step of my journey, then I go to my end willingly.

lines.speaker: Elf Hermit
lines.text: A traveller? It's been a long time since I've had any company on this little island of mine.

lines.speaker: Elf Hermit
lines.text: I fear you are at a dead end though, there's nothing of interest for you here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't be so sure. I got here by following a clue, a clue left behind by Lady Hefin. I seek her on behalf of Baxtorian himself.

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: Baxtorian? I see. Then I have good news for you human, you have found the one you seek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're Lady Hefin?

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: I am. I have lived here in solitude for a long time, but now that solitude must end. Where am I needed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The village of Lletya, north of here.

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: Then it is time to resume my role among the elves. I will go at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Head to the cave at the Iorwerth Camp and recover the Orb of Light. Be careful though, it's likely to be well guarded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello. Good work finding Lord Crwys and Lady Hefin. I never thought I'd see the other elders again, maybe we'll have a chance with them alongside us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. Any news from Ysgawyn?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: He has just returned. Let us gather the others and hear what he has to say.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Now that Ysgawyn has returned, I thought we should gather to hear what he has discovered.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Where is Arianwyn?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I saw him heading out into the forest earlier. He must have wanted some time alone.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: I hope he's okay.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
