lines.text: He'll be alright, just give him time. While he is gone, we need to keep making progress.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: On that note, what news do you have, Ysgawyn?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: The shrine at Gorlah was empty. The orb was removed quite a while ago by the looks of things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you believe me now?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: If it helps with your overinflated ego, then yes, I now believe you.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: If the orb isn't at Gorlah, Lord Iorwerth must have claimed it. It could be anywhere now.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Well luckily for us, I know exactly where it is. There's a cave just north of the Iorwerth Camp that is used to store supplies. Lord Iorwerth has it under close guard there.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: How do you know this?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Because I went scouting.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Scouting? At the Iorwerth Camp? Who gave you permission?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I gave myself permission. I do not serve you, Baxtorian, or anyone else here.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: No, you just serve yourself, don't you Ysgawyn?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: My actions benefit us all! Now that we know where the orb is, we can move to claim it.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: But why would Lord Iorwerth keep the orb at the camp? Surely it would be more secure behind the Prifddinas walls.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: That's the other thing I found out. Lord Iorwerth knows we managed to enter the library. He fears the orb would not be safe behind the walls.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: How did you discover this?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I have my methods...

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: It worries me that you are unwilling to share them.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Save your suspicions for someone who cares.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: So we know where the orb is. What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth intends to use the orb to free the dark fragment of Seren. We need to stop him.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: But this fragment of Seren, it's still part of her. Lord Iorwerth has done some unspeakable things, but he doesn't serve Zamorak like we thought, maybe we can reason with him.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I tried to reason with Lord Iorwerth a long time ago... It won't work, as much as I wish it would.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Regardless of his intentions, Lord Iorwerth is our enemy. You should want to kill him, not reason with him.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I would never wish for the death of a fellow elf, no matter what they've done.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Well look at you with your moral high ground.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Now is not the time for arguing. At some point, we will need to decide what to do about the fragment of Seren. For now though, we need to focus on getting that orb and stopping Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: The orb isn't enough for him to win though, he'll also need to break through the defences on the Temple of Light. He'll struggle to do that now he's lost Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is another way into the temple though, a tunnel from the Underground Pass, built by the dwarves there.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Great... Dwarves... I hate dwarves.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: You hate everything Ysgawyn.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth will stop at nothing to enter the temple. We have to assume that sooner or later, he will find that tunnel.

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: Then we should make sure the tunnel is secure. Baxtorian, allow myself and the other elders to go meet with these dwarves.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Very well, head to the Underground Pass and see what you can do. In the meantime, we should secure that orb. Even if he does get into the temple, Lord Iorwerth can do nothing without it.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Let me guess, you're going to send your favourite human.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Jealous, Ysgawyn?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: No. I have other things to do anyway.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: [player name], head to the cave at the Iorwerth Camp and recover the Orb of Light. Be careful though, it's likely to be well guarded.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Sounds like we're done here. Let's get moving.

sections.title: Entering the Iorwerth Camp's supply cave

lines.speaker: Iorwerth Warrior
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Tim/Sue/Sam]?

lines.speaker: Iorwerth Archer
lines.text: Well prepare to die [Tim/Sue/Sam]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh...

sections.title: Talking to Baxtorian after getting the orb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Ysgawyn has been acting suspiciously. See if you can find him and work out what he's up to. Elena said she saw him leaving the village. She might know where he went.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go talk to her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've managed to recover the orb.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Thank you friend. Without this, Lord Iorwerth has no hope of freeing that fragment of Seren. I will look after it until we decide what to do with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: We have the orb, and the elders are securing the Temple of Light as we speak. That fragment of Seren should no longer be a concern.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth is still out there and thanks to his larger army, we have no way of easily dealing with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be something we can do.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'm sure there is, but I don't know what.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps we should call the others then.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I was thinking the same. However, I have a favour to ask first. Something I'd rather keep quiet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Our group is fractured. The revelation about Seren has hit everyone hard, and opinions clearly differ on how we should be responding to it. I fear some intend to take matters into their own hands.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Ysgawyn in particular has been very distant. I have concerns about his loyalties.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't think he'd join with Lord Iorwerth do you?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I don't know. He claims to hate the Iorwerth clan, and he has more reason to feel that way than most. That said... something isn't right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you want me to investigate?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I do. Ysgawyn left the village after the meeting earlier. See if you can track him down and find out what he's up to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea where he might have gone?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'm afraid not. Elena said she saw him leaving though, maybe she can help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go talk to her.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Thanks for the talk earlier, it meant a lot. I won't keep you, I'm sure you've got things to do. See you later

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hi there. I'm sure you've got things to do. You should probably go do them.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Good to see you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Not too bad. I've been given a lot to think about though. Everything I've learnt about Seren, about the elves... it's crazy how big it all is. Our little fight in Ardougne seems so small now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm surprised it hasn't made you run for the hills.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Now you know me better than that, I'm too stubborn to leave now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you still here though?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: It's about closure I guess. So many people were hurt by Lord Iorwerth's actions in West Ardougne. I need to know that he'll never hurt them again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems to think his actions are necessary.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Maybe his cause is just, but that doesn't give him the right to ruin lives in the way he has.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think should happen to him if we win?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You mean do I think he should die?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I've tried to dedicate my life to helping people, healing them. I think all lives are valuable, even the lives of people like Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I think Lord Iorwerth should face justice for what he has done. I don't think killing him is justice though. Maybe that makes me weak, but that's how I feel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think it makes you weak, it makes you strong.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You do? Thank you, that means a lot, especially from you.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I realise I must come across as so self-righteous with all of this. I'm sorry about that.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: As much as I hate it, I know that sometimes death is the only option. I like to think we can consider alternatives though. It's what sets us apart from the enemy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He needs to pay for what he's done though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: He does, but does it need to be with his life? Killing is a solution, but maybe it's not the only one.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'm sorry. I realise I must come across as so self- righteous talking like this. I'm not trying to act better than anyone, I just want to make sure we've considered every option.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sometimes people have to die for things to change.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I suppose... I just can't help but wonder if there might be another way.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'm sorry. I realise I must come across as so self- righteous talking like this. I'm not trying to act better than anyone, I just want to make sure we've considered every option.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Anyway, did you want something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I wanted to ask you about Ysgawyn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fair. Anyway, I wanted to ask you about Ysgawyn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very true. Anyway, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Ysgawyn?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I'm trying to find him. Baxtorian said you saw him leaving the village earlier.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I did. I don't know where he went though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. That's annoying.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: He only left recently, maybe you can pick up his trail. Have a look around the hidden entrance to the village, by the wolves, and see if you can find anything out of place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea. Thanks Elena.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well well! Meeting with the Iorwerth clan, Ysgawyn? This doesn't look good for you!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Keeping an eye on you, you traitor!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Traitor? You dare call me a traitor? Your loyalties change more than the wind and you have the nerve to say I'm the traitor?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth took everything from me! My whole life was destroyed by him and that dog, Essyllt! Do you really think I'd join up with them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's what it looks like!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: You fool of a human! I may not agree much with the other rebels but everything I've done has been to stop Lord Iorwerth!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well who's this then?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Well now that you've ruined everything as usual, I suppose I'd better tell you.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: This is Iestin Edern, head of research for Lord Iorwerth, and my informant within the Iorwerth clan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... Your informant?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: That's right you idiot! Iestin here has been providing me with information on the Iorwerth clan. Not only that, he is the reason why you were able to claim that Orb of Light.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... I don't understand. If you've always been loyal, why all the secrecy?

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: That was my request. I feared that the other rebels may not be trusting of me. They could have imprisoned me or even killed me. It seemed safer to keep things secret.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Not to mention the fact that it kept Iestin safe from any informants that Lord Iorwerth might have among the rebels.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I see... I guess that all makes sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry Ysgawyn.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I don't want an apology! I don't care how you feel about me. All I care about is stopping Lord Iorwerth for good. I lost all that I had, all that I cared about! I will make him pay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn told me what they did. I...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: No! You don't get to talk about that! You have no right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry...

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: Sorry to interrupt, but I think you need to hear this.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Yes. What news do you bring Iestin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what have you been discussing?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Before you interrupted, Iestin was about to give me some news. Go ahead Iestin.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: It's not good I'm afraid. The human here was not wrong to suspect a betrayal. Lord Iorwerth, he knows about Lletya.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: By Seren! How?

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: There is a traitor amongst your people. I know not who they are, but it wasn't this human as you feared. They told Lord Iorwerth how to find the village.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: When did he find out?

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: Earlier today. Essyllt is preparing their forces as we speak.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Essyllt!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... If the traitor was one of the rebels, they'll also have told Lord Iorwerth about the tunnel into the Temple of Light!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: He'll know how to get in... He just needs to reclaim the orb! We need to get to Lletya now!

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: What about me?

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Your days as an informant are done. You're coming with us. Now we need to move!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Iona
lines.text: I... I'm scared...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have to be brave. Your mother is outside waiting for you.

lines.speaker: Iona
lines.text: Okay... I can be brave!

lines.speaker: Eoin
lines.text: What's happening? Where's mummy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's outside, can you get to her?

lines.speaker: Eoin
lines.text: I... I think so...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, you can do this!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'm fine. We need to get the citizens to safety!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Where's Baxtorian? Where's the orb?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Sorry, but I don't know.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Dammit! Right, you two go with Elena and help the citizens. I'll find Baxtorian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: But nothing! Now go!

lines.speaker: Mawrth
lines.text: My children! Someone help me find my children!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name], you go help Mawrth. We'll get the other citizens to safety.

lines.speaker: Mawrth
lines.text: My children are hiding in there! Please find them! Please save them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Go! I'll get Mawrth and the other citizens to safety!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Well well, the great King Baxtorian. I wasn't sure I believed it when I heard you were still alive, but here you are.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'm sorry to disappoint you.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Well your ability to disappoint is one of your few talents, so I'll let it slide. However, I must insist that you give me the orb.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: Crawl back into the hole you came from Lord Iorwerth, you won't leave here with that orb!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Oh I will, one way or another. But in case it wasn't clear, let me explain the situation.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: As we speak, my forces are dealing with the last of your rebels and rounding up your citizens. You have no way to fight, and nowhere to run.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Of course, I could just have you all killed right now. After the trouble you've all caused me, it wouldn't be an unreasonable act. However, I am not without mercy, and I will not draw blood unless I have to.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Instead, I give you a choice. Give me the orb, and I will withdraw my forces, leaving you in peace. Refuse, and the blood of those citizens will be on your hands.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: We do not barter with your kind!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I feared as much. That said, I do have someone here who might change your mind.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Arianwyn!

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: What is this? What have you done?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: What have I done? Well I told him the truth. It was about time that someone did.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: What truth?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: They're not the enemy. I thought they were, for so long, but they're not.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Everything we have done, every sacrifice we have made, all of it has been to free our goddess.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: You've never sacrificed, you've never lost anything. You only sacrifice those around you...

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: You have some nerve to lecture me. Remind me, how many of your people died under your watch? How many fell, trying to free your wife?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: These new followers of yours, do they know? Do they know what you did? Do they know about the monster you truly are?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I never...

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: No... of course they don't. You're a liar and a coward, that's all you've ever been.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: As for me, I'm many things, and I admit, a lot of them aren't good. But I would never throw lives away like you did.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: What's he on about?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Nothing... He's lying. He's just trying to justify his mistakes.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I've done many terrible things, but none of them were a mistake. Every sacrifice I've made has been for her, for Seren.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: And what are these apparent sacrifices you've made? Nothing compared to the suffering you've caused me, I'm sure!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: When I first discovered the truth, our clan was still ruled by my mother. I went to her, told her what I'd found, told her what we needed to do.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I loved my mother, but she was blindly loyal to this fool here, she wouldn't have any of it. I couldn't just abandon my goddess though, she needed us. So I did what had to be done.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: You killed her?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: And I would do it again without hesitation. For Seren, there is no price too great.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: You're a monster! Surely you don't mean to side with this barbarian, Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I don't condone what he did, what any of them have done. But they want what we want, we have to try working together.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: I'll die before working with them.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: No one else needs to die here. Please, just give me the orb. Do that, and I swear we will do you no further harm.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... I can't.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I see. I did hope it wouldn't come to this, but you've given me no choice. Morvran.

lines.speaker: Eoin
lines.text: What's going on? Where's mummy?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I'll make this simple for you, Baxtorian. The life of the boy is now in your hands. Give me the orb, and no harm will come to him.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: You wouldn't...

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: No, I wouldn't. But the choice is not mine, it's yours. I will count to five.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: One.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth, please...

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Two.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Baxtorian, please. Just give him the orb.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Three.

lines.speaker: Eoin
lines.text: I don't like this, I want mummy!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Four.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Stop!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'll give you the orb. Just please, don't hurt anyone else.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: A wise choice, must be a first for you.

lines.speaker: Ysgawyn
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Ysgawyn!

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Get after them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn...

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: It wasn't meant to go like this. No one was meant to get hurt. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand... why?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: For so long I was led to believe that Lord Iorwerth was our enemy. I thought he'd turned his back on Seren, thought he'd betrayed her.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: But I was wrong. Everything we thought we were fighting for was a lie. He never betrayed her, everything he did was to free her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whatever it is he's trying to free... it isn't her.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: How do you know? Because of what you found in the library? How do we know that was her? How do we know what's true anymore?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that's why you turned to Lord Iorwerth? To find out the truth?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: My whole life I've dreamed of her, dreamed of what it would be like to meet her. If there's even a chance that she's still alive, I have to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And if she is alive? Then what?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Seren guided the elves for thousands of years. With her back, we'll finally be able to reform Prifddinas. We'll finally have peace again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Peace? Look around you Arianwyn. Look at what Lord Iorwerth has done to this village, your home. Does this look like the work of someone who wants peace?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I... I don't...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It doesn't have to be this way. Step aside. Let me stop Lord Iorwerth and reclaim the orb. Once he's dealt with, we can find out the truth, our way.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: You... you know I can't do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn please...

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I'm sorry friend, but I've made my choice. Now please, walk away. No one else needs to die here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't let him get away.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: And I can't let you go after him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So be it.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Ysgawyn...

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: He wasn't a friend, to any of us. Everything he cared about he lost a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: But he didn't give up. To the very end, he fought for a cause he believed in, our cause.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: And now he's gone. Along with the orb and that traitor, Arianwyn. Lord Iorwerth has everything he needs now. He'll be preparing to move on the temple as we speak.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: We can't give up Islwyn. If we do, then he died for nothing.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Oh I don't intend to give up, but make no mistake, this is a fight we're unlikely to win.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: So what do we do? Baxtorian?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... I don't think there's anything we can do. Lord Iorwerth will destroy us if we try to stop him.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Maybe, but we have to try. What else is there?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: We'd be throwing lives away...

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Well Lord Iorwerth seemed to think you were no stranger to that. Why did he think that?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I don't know, probably just some lie he tells himself.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Hmm. I don't believe you.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: You'd trust Lord Iorwerth over me?

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: I wouldn't trust Lord Iorwerth if my life depended on it. I know a liar when I see one though, and I can tell you're not being honest about something.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: What happened? Tell us.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: It was after we were defeated by Lord Iorwerth. With Prifddinas lost to us, we returned to our kingdom in the east. We'd been gone for years by then, and in our absence, the kingdom had fallen to our other foes.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: So many dead... my people, the ones I was meant to protect. But that wasn't the worst of it. Glarial, my beloved, she was taken from me.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: We searched far and wide, desperate to find her. At times it felt like we were close, but the enemy always managed to elude us.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Eventually, we managed to track her captors to some caves south of our ruined kingdom. It was clearly a trap, but I was a fool. I was so blind in my desire to find her, I didn't consider the obvious.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I sent my soldiers in, the few that I still had. They fought the best they could, but it was hopeless. They were massacred.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: After that, I knew I couldn't carry on. I was alone and powerless. So I sealed myself away, in the hope that one day she would come back to me.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: You... you killed them? You killed them all?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I did. But you don't understand, I loved my wife. If there was a chance of freeing her, I had to take it.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: You think I don't understand love? I knew love, for Eirlys! My mother! Your daughter! She died in your war, while you hid away!

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth was right! You're just a coward!

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Islwyn, where are you going?

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: The Underground Pass! I intend to fight for my people, even if their own king won't!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Do you need to hear one of the clues again?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I used to think I was a good king, one that took care of his people. Thanks to me, they had roofs over their heads and food for their tables.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: And yet... I never loved them, not really. The only thing I loved was her, my Glarial.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: When they took her from me... I was torn apart. I vowed I would get her back no matter the cost. That vow destroyed my people... I destroyed my people. In the end... I became just like Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you did fail your people, you didn't destroy them though.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Maybe not then... but now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think this is over? Is that it? Lord Iorwerth beats us once so we just give up? If you truly think that then yes, you have destroyed your people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want to just rot here? Wallowing in your own self pity while you lose the little you still have?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: What other option is there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We can fight! Lord Iorwerth doesn't win until he gets into the Temple of Light! We can still stop this!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: But he outnumbers us ten to one... We'll all die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely it's better to die trying than to live a life of regret? I'd have thought you'd know that better than anyone.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... I can't...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh for crying out loud! You have got to be the biggest waste of space I have ever met! I've met goblins with more of a spine than you! For once in your miserable life why don't you actually do something worthwhile?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: You... You're awful at pep talks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't meant to be a pep talk!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh* Look... Ask yourself this... What would she want?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: She... She'd want me to save them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why are we still stood around talking? We've got work to do!

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... You're right... We don't have a plan though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth only has one way into the Temple of Light, the dwarf tunnel from the Underground Pass. We need to stop him getting through.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The dwarves should be able to seal the tunnel. We'll need to defend them while they do.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: But we don't have the strength to withstand Lord Iorwerth's forces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We have the clan elders, their combined power is an army in itself.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Not all the clan elders. We're still missing two.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well have you deciphered their clues?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I have, but there's a problem. We should hopefully be able to find Lady Meilyr. But Lady Ithell, she died in the early days of the war.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not good. Did she have any descendants?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I had a look. Kelyn Ithell is her closest living relative. Problem is, we don't just need a Lady Ithell, we need the Lady Ithell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. How did she die?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: An illness of some sort, I never actually knew what it was. She'd had it for years.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For years? So she'd have been ill when she gave you her clue. If she knew she was dying, maybe her clue reflects that.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Knowledge can be passed on. Maybe the clue will lead us to her knowledge.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I... I suppose you could be right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's worth a shot at least. Leave it with me and I'll see what I can do. In the meantime, you should take the others to the dwarf camp in the Underground Pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See if you can get in contact with General Hining and the new king of Ardougne as well. Maybe they'll be willing to help us.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'll see what I can do. We'll need to work quickly though, Lord Iorwerth will be preparing his forces as we speak. Now I guess you'll be wanting to hear those clues?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's Lady Ithell's clue?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Here is the clue she left behind: 'I'll put my soul into my greatest work.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The Ithell clan is a clan of builders and architects. Before her death, Lady Ithell was considered the finest architect of all the elves. This village was her final and finest contribution to our people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's Lady Meilyr's clue?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Here is the clue she left behind: 'Follow the scrolls to find me.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The Meilyr clan is a clan of scientists and explorers. You'll probably need to do some exploring yourself to find Lady Meilyr. You may also find this of use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still here, Iestin?

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: Well I can't really go back to Lord Iorwerth now. He knows I betrayed him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but you don't have to stay here.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: I've made my decision and I intend to stick by it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why follow him in the first place?

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: After we lost Seren, Baxtorian was chosen to lead us. Rather than looking after the city of Prifddinas, he chose to expand to the east, leaving us behind.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: With no ruler, Prifddinas fell into chaos. When Lord Iorwerth came to us and said he'd found a way to bring Seren back to us, you can understand why we turned to him.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: I wish things were as simple as good against evil, but that's rarely the case.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose you're right. Anyway, I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena. What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Making sure I've got everything ready. I'll be leaving for the Underground Pass soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're coming with us?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I've stuck around for this long, it would feel wrong to leave now. Besides, I may not be much of a fighter, but I can help in other ways.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very true. I'll leave you to prepare.

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: Oh wow!

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: I... I see it! I see everything! All of her knowledge, I have it now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lady Ithell's knowledge?

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: Yes, I know everything that she knew! The statue, it must have given it to me somehow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're the closest living relative of Lady Ithell. Now you have her knowledge as well. I suppose that makes you the new Lady Ithell.

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: I... I suppose it does. Wow!

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: I guess you'll be wanting me to help defend the Underground Pass then? Well don't worry, I was planning on joining you anyway. With this new knowledge, I can be an even bigger help.

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: I'll head there right away. Time is of the essence.

lines.speaker: Lady Ffion Meilyr
lines.text: At last Baxtorian calls to me. I can't say I expected him to send a human, but I suppose nothing is as expected these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need your help. The final fight against Lord Iorwerth is nearly here.

lines.speaker: Lady Ffion Meilyr
lines.text: I will join you in this darkest of hours. Where must I go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The battle will take place in the Underground Pass. Head to Lletya first though, Baxtorian will meet you there.

lines.speaker: Lady Ffion Meilyr
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh... Hello. Most of the others have departed for the Underground Pass. I'll be leaving with the rest soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I shall meet you there.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Don't take too long. We'll be at the dwarf camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you Islwyn?

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: As good as can be, all things considered. I idolised my grandfather for years. They always say you should never meet your heroes though, now I see why.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's still your family though.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: He is, he's the only family I have left. The rest of them died in his war. A war that will finally end today.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Maybe if we both survive, I'll find it in my heart to forgive him. But for now, I have to focus elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you to prepare.

lines.speaker: Islwyn
lines.text: Good luck [player name]. I'll see you on the other side.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Arianwyn is out there, walking alongside our greatest enemy.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: He saved me once you know. Some of Lord Iorwerth's men found me in the forest. There were too many for me to fight off, I thought I was done for. Without Arianwyn, I wouldn't be here today.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: But now he fights alongside them. This war has torn us apart, all of us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it ends today.

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Yes, today it ends... For better, or for worse.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: I wonder if Essyllt will join the battle today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do if he does?

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Me? Nothing. I'm not much of a fighter. I'll be helping with the wounded instead.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: You though... If you see him, don't show him mercy, for he will show you none. Instead, you should show him what happens to those who hurt us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For Ysgawyn?

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: No one liked him that much, but he was one of us, and he died trying to save us. He deserves justice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I get the chance, I'll make sure he gets it.

lines.speaker: Ilfeen
lines.text: Thank you friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: Those are my people coming for us. My brothers and sisters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They were once, but not anymore. You're one of us now.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: He's not evil you know, Lord Iorwerth. None of them are really, apart from Essyllt maybe.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: Those that follow him, they do so because they believe in him, believe in what he's doing. For a long time, I believed in him as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why did you betray him?

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: He became so obsessed with his cause, he forgot why we started following him in the first place. He became more extreme, more radical.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: I tried to advise him as best I could, but over time he started listening more to Essyllt and less to me.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: After I lost my brother, who died following Lord Iorwerth's orders, I realised he needed to be stopped before it was too late.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You made the right choice.

lines.speaker: Iestin
lines.text: I hope so. But even now I find myself filled with doubt.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Nothing like a *hic* bit of home brew before getting to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure you should be drinking? We can't afford to make mistakes here.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Argh, I work better with a bit of *hic* brew in me. Nothing for you to *hic* worry about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're sure.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Didn't think I'd see you back down here [player name]. The elves are lucky to have you on their side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're lucky to have you as well, you're our last hope.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: What an awful thought. Four dwarves, one a drunk, are the final hope against this darkness. I hope we don't let you down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You won't.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: I hope you're right friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take care Klank.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: You too adventurer...

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: [player name]. We'll soon be ready to seal the tunnel, it's slow work I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: If we do this wrong, we'll bring the entire cavern down upon all of us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that would definitely stop Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: It would. I don't know about you though, but I intend to survive this day. Bringing the cavern down isn't a good way to achieve that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: True that. I'll leave you to your work.

lines.speaker: Thorgel
lines.text: Hey there [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's it going Thorgel?

lines.speaker: Thorgel
lines.text: Apart from the army of elves about to descend on us, pretty good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll be fine, you've got all of us to defend you.

lines.speaker: Thorgel
lines.text: Well let's hope your better at fighting than Kamen is at brewing, or this will be a short battle.

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: So here we are. At long last, the end is upon us. The only question that remains is who it's the end for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You knew this day would come?

lines.speaker: Lord Ieuan Amlodd
lines.text: No war lasts forever, not even this one. The darkest verse of the song of the elves is upon us. Who knows what the next verse will be.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth better show up soon! I can't wait forever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem keen.

lines.speaker: Lady Tangwen Trahaearn
lines.text: I've lived for thousands of years. If I'm going to die today, I'd rather just get on with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: This is a dark place we've come to. Fitting I suppose, for this is to be our darkest hour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think the fragment of Seren is the cause of the darkness?

lines.speaker: Lord Piquan Crwys
lines.text: Possibly, I suspect it was here long before her though. And whatever happens today, I'm sure it will remain for long after.

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: The greatest battle of our time is almost upon us. We should take the time to reflect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've never really been one for looking backwards. I prefer to look ahead.

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: You can still reflect on the future as well as the past. If we survive today, our future will be changed forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose. I'll probably wait until we've won to think about that though.

lines.speaker: Lady Carys Hefin
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: I hope we win today. It would be a bit embarrassing to be known as the shortest serving clan elder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bet. I'd also like to avoid death if possible. I suspect it's not very fun.

lines.speaker: Lady Kelyn Ithell
lines.text: Not much is fun when Lord Iorwerth is involved. If all goes well though, he won't be a problem after today.

lines.speaker: Lady Ffion Meilyr
lines.text: I've been on the run from Lord Iorwerth for centuries. I'll be glad to finally see peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you think we'll win?

lines.speaker: Lady Ffion Meilyr
lines.text: Don't forget that death is a form of peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess. I'd rather avoid it if I can though.

lines.speaker: Lady Ffion Meilyr
lines.text: There are worse things in this world than death. Never forget that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to see you General.

lines.speaker: General Hining
lines.text: And you friend. It will be an honour to fight against the darkness alongside you. Together, we can avenge King Tyras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem well prepared for the battle, compared to the rest of us at least.

lines.speaker: General Hining
lines.text: We knew this battle would come, ever since the day our king died. We've been prepared for a long time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope it's enough.

lines.speaker: General Hining
lines.text: As do I. If it's not though, we'll make sure they remember us until the end of their days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That we will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Speak with Elena. She said she had an idea that might help our defence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Ah... You made it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That I did. I see you managed to gather some allies.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Yes, General Hining has given us the full support of the Tyras forces. Sadly though, the new king of Ardougne does not wish to assist us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Typical, after all we did for them.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I suppose they've had enough of elves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. How are the defences looking?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: We've managed to fortify the area around the camp quite well. The dwarves have set up some cannons and General Hining's men have built some catapults.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good. We may be outnumbered, but the defences will give us a fighting chance.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I hope so... I fear it won't be enough to win though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't need to win, we just need to buy the dwarves enough time to seal that tunnel. Do we know how long they'll need?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: They haven't given us an exact time, it's a slow process apparently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I suppose we don't want them to do it wrong, they could bring the entire cavern down on top of us. Do we know where Lord Iorwerth is?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: The scouts have reported that his forces are on the move as we speak. It won't be long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's use the time wisely then. Is there anything I can do before the attack begins?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Elena said she had an idea that might help our defence. You could talk to her and see if she needs any help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey. I think Baxtorian wants to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: So this is it, after everything that's happened, this is where it ends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you afraid?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I... I am.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What about you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid too.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You... You matter to me you know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You matter to me as well.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: [player name]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Don't die today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Thank you [player name], you're a good friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not afraid.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I... I'm terrible at these moments. I never know what to say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I care about you Elena, you know that right?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Thank you [player name], you're a good friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I don't know what to say either.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Thank you [player name], you're a good friend.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Good luck today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Baxtorian said you had an idea to help our defence.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: That's right. One of the dwarves here, Kamen, has brewed up some strange alcohol. Whatever it is, it's very flammable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I've made good use of it in the past.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I think we can make use of it again. I've combined the alcohol with some other chemicals. The result is quite explosive!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: If we position some of these explosives on the approach to the camp, we can use them against the Iorwerth forces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, that could work quite well. Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I've dug some holes around the camp for the explosives to go in. Could you fill them in for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Here's the explosives. If you need more just let me know.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Elena.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you afraid?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I... I am.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What about you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid too.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Good luck today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid too.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Good luck today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the last of them. I'd better get back to Baxtorian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that's everything prepared.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Good, because we're out of time. A message came into the camp, Lord Iorwerth wishes to meet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wants to meet? Surely it's a trap?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Maybe, but I don't think so. Even if nothing came of it, he was willing to meet me before the Siege of Prifddinas all those years ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you going to do?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I will go. If there's a chance to avoid bloodshed, we should take it, no matter how slim the odds are. I would like you to join me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think Lord Iorwerth will listen to me?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: No, but maybe Arianwyn will. He considered you a friend, he may still care for what you have to say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Then I will join you.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Thank you friend. Now, Lord Iorwerth is unlikely to be patient, but we can spare a bit of time. If there's anyone you wish to talk to here, I suggest you do so before we depart. You may not get another chance.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Let me know when you're ready to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Then we will leave at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Don't take too long. I'll be here when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: King Baxtorian.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'm not a king... not anymore.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: No. You never really were a king though were you? A king doesn't abandon his people like you did.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I did not come here to trade insults.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Really? So why did you come then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We came to put an end to this. To put aside our differences and stop this war before more people die needlessly.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: You talk sense human, and yet you stand beside the warmonger himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have a fair share of blood on your hands as well you know.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I do. I have done many unspeakable things, things that haunt me every day. But what I did, I did for the good of my people.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Whereas he, he only ever cared for himself.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Go on, deny it. Just like you always do.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I don't deny it. I failed my people, I failed you. I was never a worthy king, I know that now. But you can't use my failures as a mask to hide your own.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: We can stand here going around in circles all day, you won't change my mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're not willing to change your mind, why even bother to meet us?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: In the hope that I might still change yours. I don't want anyone else to die in this war. I give you the chance to stand down.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: And what happens if we do?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: We enter the temple, free our goddess and with her by our side, reform the city of Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: And we all live happily ever after?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: In a manner of speaking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That temple, it doesn't contain your goddess. At best, it contains a shadow of what she once was.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Yes, Arianwyn told me what you discovered in the library. It matters not though, that is a part of our goddess in there, we have to free her.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: And if she's evil? What do you do then?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: My loyalty is to Seren, I will let her decide what is evil.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: That... that's not what we agreed.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: We agreed to enter the temple and free Seren. If that's not Seren in there, we shouldn't free her.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I will keep my promise to you Arianwyn, you should not doubt that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why shouldn't he doubt that? You burnt his home, you killed his friends. You haven't given him much reason to trust you so far.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I did what I needed to do to save our goddess. No one would have needed to die had you not forced our hand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Listen to him Arianwyn, he'd sacrifice everything and everyone just to free whatever is in that temple. Is that really what you want?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Enough of this! You can try to manipulate him all you want, he's made his choice.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Now, I'm afraid I'll need to push you on a decision.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I'm sorry, but we can't let you enter that temple.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: A shame. As unlikely as it was, I did hope you might see reason.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I think we're done here then. For what it's worth, I'm sorry it had to end this way.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: As am I.

lines.speaker: Rebel Warrior
lines.text: Iorwerth forces approaching!

sections.title: Upon successfully defending the Dwarf Camp

lines.speaker: Rebel Warrior
lines.text: Incoming!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena!

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Ha! Looks like you were too slow my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Get away from her!

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Or what? Will you murder me in cold blood like you did with King Tyras?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's only one murderer here!

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: You can lie to yourself all you want, I know what you are. You enjoy killing, just like I do.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: The satisfaction when I finally put an end to that fool Ysgawyn, it was beautiful. It was even better than when I dealt with that stupid lover of his.

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: But if that wasn't enough, now I get to be the one to finally put an end to you and your pathetic girlfriend!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that!

lines.speaker: Essyllt
lines.text: Oh I'm going to enjoy this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This can't be good...

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Go! Get after them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about you?

lines.speaker: Eluned
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll take care of her.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Good luck [player name].

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: This is it, this is what we've spent the last thousand years fighting for. Today, it will finally all be worth it.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth please, see reason. That thing is not Seren, it's not our goddess.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: And what would you know?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: I knew Seren, I'm the only one here that did, and I know that isn't her.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Time can change a lot of things, perhaps Seren has changed as well.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I... I don't think it's her.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Of course it's her! Don't tell me you believe Baxtorian and his lies?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I may not have met our goddess, but I heard enough stories to know what she looked like. This thing, it's not her.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: Well lucky for us, we have a way of finding out.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Lord Iorwerth please, this isn't what we agreed.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I know what we agreed Arianwyn! We agreed to free our goddess, and that's what I'm going to do!

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Fragment of Seren
lines.text: You... you've come to free me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on? Why's everything so dark?

lines.speaker: Fragment of Seren
lines.text: Dark? All I've ever known is darkness. It's all she gave me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seren?

lines.speaker: Fragment of Seren
lines.text: You know of her? How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Fragment of Seren
lines.text: You haven't come to free me at all have you? You're one of her servants!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not quite...

lines.speaker: Fragment of Seren
lines.text: I loved her! She made me! But I couldn't be what she wanted, so she abandoned me! And now she can't bear to kill me herself, so she sends you instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't...

lines.speaker: Fragment of Seren
lines.text: I won't let you! It's not fair! She doesn't get to do this! I don't want it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait!

lines.speaker: Fragment of Seren
lines.text: No! Get out! Get out! Get out!

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: What... what have you done?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: She was confused, she was hurt. Yes, she was angry, but she was innocent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I tried to stop her...

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: How? With violence? That's all you know isn't it?

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: She just needed time, time to understand, time to overcome her pain. She was our goddess, the last hope for our people. And now, she's gone, because of you.

lines.speaker: Lord Iorwerth
lines.text: I hope you can live with yourself...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arianwyn.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: As well as can be expected. My guilt is a heavy load. A lot of people died because of me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A lot more might have died if you hadn't stopped Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: He wasn't evil, not truly. He just became so obsessed with his goal that he forgot why he started fighting in the first place.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: As for the fragment of Seren... I wish there had been another way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She wasn't Seren, and she never would have been.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: No, and at least now her pain has ended. Still, I'll always wonder what could have been...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do now?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: There's no place for me here anymore, not after what I've done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're leaving?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I wanted to stay long enough to help repair the damage of my actions, and to look upon Prifddinas for the first time. I've done that now. Lletya has been rebuilt and Prifddinas has been reformed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where will you go?

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: I don't know. There's a lot of places on this world that I've never seen. Maybe one of them will help me find redemption, and forgiveness.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: The elves here will do fine. Baxtorian has his own path of redemption to go on, and I imagine he'll struggle far more than me. He has the other elders to support him though. Under them, our people will prosper.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: Anyway, you should get going. Baxtorian will be waiting for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll miss you.

lines.speaker: Arianwyn
lines.text: And I you. Farewell friend.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. Welcome.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, good to see you both.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: And you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought the city had been restored, I don't see anything though.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: One of the security measures put in place during the God Wars, from the outside it seems like there's nothing there. Don't worry though, the restoration is complete.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Speaking of which, myself and the other elders have come to an agreement. For too long, we've been afraid of outsiders, showing them mistrust while our own kind plot against us.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: After all that's happened, we've decided that from now on, that will no longer be the case. We will open our city up to the other races, letting them walk among us for the first time.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: As a thank you for all that you've done, we'd like the two of you to be the first to enter Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's quite the honour.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: They've also agreed to let me move into the city. That tiny house in Ardougne was becoming a bit tiresome, and there's so much for me to learn from the Meilyr elves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll be sure to come and visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that the city has been reformed, are you going to become king of the elves again?

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: Oh I said my days as a king were done, and I meant it. From now on, the elves will be jointly ruled by the eight clan elders. No single elf will ever rule again. We've realised that we're not overly good at it.

lines.speaker: Baxtorian
lines.text: But anyway, we can talk more later. For now, I'd say it's time for the two of you to see Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Ready [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do this.
